     104                 :            : /*
     105                 :            :  *              Hash tables that index the relation cache
     106                 :            :  *
     107                 :            :  *              We used to index the cache by both name and OID, but now there
     108                 :            :  *              is only an index by OID.
     109                 :            :  */
     110                 :            : typedef struct relidcacheent
     111                 :            : {
     112                 :            :         Oid                     reloid;
     113                 :            :         Relation        reldesc;
     114                 :            : } RelIdCacheEnt;
     115                 :            : 
     116                 :            : static HTAB *RelationIdCache;
     117                 :            : 
     118                 :            : /*
     119                 :            :  * This flag is false until we have prepared the critical relcache entries
     120                 :            :  * that are needed to do indexscans on the tables read by relcache building.
     121                 :            :  */
     122                 :            : bool            criticalRelcachesBuilt = false;
     123                 :            : 
     124                 :            : /*
     125                 :            :  * This flag is false until we have prepared the critical relcache entries
     126                 :            :  * for shared catalogs (which are the tables needed for login).
     127                 :            :  */
     128                 :            : bool            criticalSharedRelcachesBuilt = false;
     129                 :            : 
     130                 :            : /*
     131                 :            :  * This counter counts relcache inval events received since backend startup
     132                 :            :  * (but only for rels that are actually in cache).      Presently, we use it only
     133                 :            :  * to detect whether data about to be written by write_relcache_init_file()
     134                 :            :  * might already be obsolete.
     135                 :            :  */
     136                 :            : static long relcacheInvalsReceived = 0L;
     137                 :            : 
     138                 :            : /*
     139                 :            :  * This list remembers the OIDs of the non-shared relations cached in the
     140                 :            :  * database's local relcache init file.  Note that there is no corresponding
     141                 :            :  * list for the shared relcache init file, for reasons explained in the
     142                 :            :  * comments for RelationCacheInitFileRemove.
     143                 :            :  */
     144                 :            : static List *initFileRelationIds = NIL;

     147                 :            :  * This flag lets us optimize away work in AtEO(Sub)Xact_RelationCache().

     149                 :            : static bool need_eoxact_work = false;

     152                 :            : /*
     153                 :            :  *              macros to manipulate the lookup hashtables
     154                 :            :  */
     155                 :            : #define RelationCacheInsert(RELATION)   \
     156                 :            : do { \
     157                 :            :         RelIdCacheEnt *idhentry; bool found; \
     158                 :            :         idhentry = (RelIdCacheEnt*)hash_search(RelationIdCache, \
     159                 :            :                                                                                    (void *) &(RELATION->rd_id), \
     160                 :            :                                                                                    HASH_ENTER, &found); \
     161                 :            :         /* used to give notice if found -- now just keep quiet */ \
     162                 :            :         idhentry->reldesc = RELATION; \
     163                 :            : } while(0)
     164                 :            : 
     165                 :            : #define RelationIdCacheLookup(ID, RELATION) \
     166                 :            : do { \
     167                 :            :         RelIdCacheEnt *hentry; \
     168                 :            :         hentry = (RelIdCacheEnt*)hash_search(RelationIdCache, \
     169                 :            :                                                                                  (void *) &(ID), \
     170                 :            :                                                                                  HASH_FIND, NULL); \
     171                 :            :         if (hentry) \
     172                 :            :                 RELATION = hentry->reldesc; \
     173                 :            :         else \
     174                 :            :                 RELATION = NULL; \
     175                 :            : } while(0)
     176                 :            : 
     177                 :            : #define RelationCacheDelete(RELATION) \
     178                 :            : do { \
     179                 :            :         RelIdCacheEnt *idhentry; \
     180                 :            :         idhentry = (RelIdCacheEnt*)hash_search(RelationIdCache, \
     181                 :            :                                                                                    (void *) &(RELATION->rd_id), \
     182                 :            :                                                                                    HASH_REMOVE, NULL); \
     183                 :            :         if (idhentry == NULL) \
     184                 :            :                 elog(WARNING, "trying to delete a rd_id reldesc that does not exist"); \
     185                 :            : } while(0)

     188                 :            : /*
     189                 :            :  * Special cache for opclass-related information
     190                 :            :  *
     191                 :            :  * Note: only default operators and support procs get cached, ie, those with
     192                 :            :  * lefttype = righttype = opcintype.
     193                 :            :  */
     194                 :            : typedef struct opclasscacheent
     195                 :            : {
     196                 :            :         Oid                     opclassoid;             /* lookup key: OID of opclass */
     197                 :            :         bool            valid;                  /* set TRUE after successful fill-in */
     198                 :            :         StrategyNumber numStrats;       /* max # of strategies (from pg_am) */
     199                 :            :         StrategyNumber numSupport;      /* max # of support procs (from pg_am) */
     200                 :            :         Oid                     opcfamily;              /* OID of opclass's family */
     201                 :            :         Oid                     opcintype;              /* OID of opclass's declared input type */
     202                 :            :         Oid                *operatorOids;       /* strategy operators' OIDs */
     203                 :            :         RegProcedure *supportProcs; /* support procs */
     204                 :            : } OpClassCacheEnt;

     243                 :            : 
     244                 :            : /*
     245                 :            :  *              ScanPgRelation
     246                 :            :  *
     247                 :            :  *              This is used by RelationBuildDesc to find a pg_class
     248                 :            :  *              tuple matching targetRelId.  The caller must hold at least
     249                 :            :  *              AccessShareLock on the target relid to prevent concurrent-update
     250                 :            :  *              scenarios --- else our SnapshotNow scan might fail to find any
     251                 :            :  *              version that it thinks is live.
     252                 :            :  *
     253                 :            :  *              NB: the returned tuple has been copied into palloc'd storage
     254                 :            :  *              and must eventually be freed with heap_freetuple.
     255                 :            :  */
     256                 :            : static HeapTuple
     257                 :            : ScanPgRelation(Oid targetRelId, bool indexOK, Relation *pg_class_relation)
     258                 :            : {
     259                 :            :         HeapTuple       pg_class_tuple;
     260                 :            :         Relation        pg_class_desc;
     261                 :            :         SysScanDesc pg_class_scan;
     262                 :            :         ScanKeyData key[1];
     263                 :            : 
     264                 :            :         /*
     265                 :            :          * If something goes wrong during backend startup, we might find ourselves
     266                 :            :          * trying to read pg_class before we've selected a database.  That ain't
     267                 :            :          * gonna work, so bail out with a useful error message.  If this happens,
     268                 :            :          * it probably means a relcache entry that needs to be nailed isn't.
     269                 :            :          */
     270         [ -  + ]:    1105876 :         if (!OidIsValid(MyDatabaseId))
     271                 :          0 :                 elog(FATAL, "cannot read pg_class without having selected a database");
     272                 :            : 
     273                 :            :         /*
     274                 :            :          * form a scan key
     275                 :            :          */
     276                 :    1105876 :         ScanKeyInit(&key[0],
     277                 :            :                                 ObjectIdAttributeNumber,
     278                 :            :                                 BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
     279                 :    1105876 :                                 ObjectIdGetDatum(targetRelId));
     280                 :            : 
     281                 :            :         /*
     282                 :            :          * Open pg_class and fetch a tuple.  Force heap scan if we haven't yet
     283                 :            :          * built the critical relcache entries (this includes initdb and startup
     284                 :            :          * without a pg_internal.init file).  The caller can also force a heap
     285                 :            :          * scan by setting indexOK == false.
     286                 :            :          */
     287                 :    1105876 :         pg_class_desc = heap_open(RelationRelationId, AccessShareLock);
     288                 :    1105876 :         pg_class_scan = systable_beginscan(pg_class_desc, ClassOidIndexId,
     289         [ +  + ]:    1105876 :                                                                            indexOK && criticalRelcachesBuilt,
     290                 :            :                                                                            SnapshotNow,
     291                 :    1105876 :                                                                            1, key);
     292                 :            : 
     293                 :    1105876 :         pg_class_tuple = systable_getnext(pg_class_scan);
     294                 :            : 
     295                 :            :         /*
     296                 :            :          * Must copy tuple before releasing buffer.
     297                 :            :          */
     298         [ +  - ]:    1105876 :         if (HeapTupleIsValid(pg_class_tuple))
     299                 :    1105876 :                 pg_class_tuple = heap_copytuple(pg_class_tuple);
     300                 :            : 
     301                 :            :         /* all done */
     302                 :    1105876 :         systable_endscan(pg_class_scan);
     303         [ +  + ]:    1105876 :         if (pg_class_relation == NULL)
     304                 :     224700 :                 heap_close(pg_class_desc, AccessShareLock);
     305                 :            :         else
     306                 :     881176 :                 *pg_class_relation = pg_class_desc;
     307                 :            : 
     308                 :    1105876 :         return pg_class_tuple;
     309                 :            : }

     445                 :            :         /*
     446                 :            :          * Open gp_relation_node and fetch a tuple.  Force heap scan if we haven't yet
     447                 :            :          * built the critical relcache entries (this includes initdb and startup

     456                 :     362391 :         tuple = systable_getnext(scan);

     470                 :            : HeapTuple
     471                 :            : FetchGpRelationNodeTuple(
     472                 :            :         Relation                gp_relation_node,
     473                 :            :         Oid                     tablespaceOid,
     474                 :            :         Oid                     relfilenode,
     475                 :            :         int32                   segmentFileNum,
     476                 :            :         ItemPointer             persistentTid,
     477                 :            :         int64                   *persistentSerialNum)
     478                 :            : {
     479                 :            :         HeapTuple tuple;
     480                 :            :         
     481                 :            :         bool                    nulls[Natts_gp_relation_node];
     482                 :            :         Datum                   values[Natts_gp_relation_node];
     483                 :            : 
     484                 :            :         Oid tablespace;
     485                 :            :         Oid actualRelationNode;
     486                 :            :         int32 actualSegmentFileNum;
     487                 :            : 
     488                 :            :         int64 createMirrorDataLossTrackingSessionNum;
     489                 :            : 
     490                 :            :         /*
     491                 :            :          * gp_relation_node stores tablespaceOId in pg_class fashion, hence need
     492                 :            :          * to fetch the similar way.
     493                 :            :          */
     494 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:     362391 :         Assert (tablespaceOid != MyDatabaseTableSpace);

     499                 :     362391 :                                         tablespaceOid,
     500                 :     362391 :                                         relfilenode,
     501                 :     362391 :                                         segmentFileNum);
     502                 :            :         
     503                 :            :         /*
     504                 :            :          * if no such tuple exists, return NULL

     507                 :            :         {
     508 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:      50718 :                 MemSet(persistentTid, 0, sizeof(ItemPointerData));
         [ #  # ][ #  # ]
                 [ #  # ]
     509                 :      50718 :                 *persistentSerialNum = 0;
     510                 :      50718 :                 return tuple;
     511                 :            :         }
     512                 :            :         
     513                 :     311673 :         heap_deform_tuple(tuple, RelationGetDescr(gp_relation_node), values, nulls);
     514                 :            :                 
     515                 :     311673 :         GpRelationNode_GetValues(
     516                 :     311673 :                                                 values,
     517                 :            :                                                 &tablespace,

     520                 :            :                                                 &createMirrorDataLossTrackingSessionNum,

     522                 :     311673 :                                                 persistentSerialNum);

     524         [ -  + ]:     311673 :         if (actualRelationNode != relfilenode)
     525                 :            :         {
     526                 :          0 :                 elog(ERROR, "Index on gp_relation_node broken."

     528                 :            :                          relfilenode,
     529                 :            :                          actualRelationNode);
     530                 :          0 :         }
     531                 :            : 
     532                 :     311673 :         return tuple;
     533                 :     362391 : }

     535                 :            : /*
     536                 :            :  * Deletes the gp relation node entry for the
     537                 :            :  * given segment file.
     538                 :            :  */ 
     539                 :            : void
     540                 :            : DeleteGpRelationNodeTuple(
     541                 :            :         Relation        relation,
     542                 :            :         int32           segmentFileNum)
     543                 :            : {

     554                 :      95609 :                                                                          segmentFileNum,

     557                 :            : 
     558         [ -  + ]:      95609 :         if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tuple))

     561                 :            :                          relation->rd_rel->reltablespace,
     562                 :            :                          relation->rd_rel->relfilenode,

     565                 :            :         /* delete the relation tuple from gp_relation_node, and finish up */

     567                 :      95609 :         heap_freetuple(tuple);

     760                 :            : /*
     761                 :            :  *              AllocateRelationDesc
     762                 :            :  *
     763                 :            :  *              This is used to allocate memory for a new relation descriptor
     764                 :            :  *              and initialize the rd_rel field from the given pg_class tuple.
     765                 :            :  */
     766                 :            : static Relation
     767                 :            : AllocateRelationDesc(Form_pg_class relp)
     768                 :            : {
     769                 :            :         Relation        relation;
     770                 :            :         MemoryContext oldcxt;
     771                 :            :         Form_pg_class relationForm;
     772                 :            : 
     773                 :            :         /* Relcache entries must live in CacheMemoryContext */
     774                 :     881176 :         oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(CacheMemoryContext);
     775                 :            : 
     776                 :            :         /*
     777                 :            :          * allocate and zero space for new relation descriptor
     778                 :            :          */
     779                 :     881176 :         relation = (Relation) palloc0(sizeof(RelationData));
     780                 :            : 
     781                 :            :         /*
     782                 :            :          * clear fields of reldesc that should initialize to something non-zero
     783                 :            :          */
     784                 :     881176 :         relation->rd_targblock = InvalidBlockNumber;
     785                 :            : 
     786                 :            :         /* make sure relation is marked as having no open file yet */
     787                 :     881176 :         relation->rd_smgr = NULL;
     788                 :            : 
     789                 :            :         /*
     790                 :            :          * Copy the relation tuple form
     791                 :            :          *
     792                 :            :          * We only allocate space for the fixed fields, ie, CLASS_TUPLE_SIZE. The
     793                 :            :          * variable-length fields (relacl, reloptions) are NOT stored in the
     794                 :            :          * relcache --- there'd be little point in it, since we don't copy the
     795                 :            :          * tuple's nulls bitmap and hence wouldn't know if the values are valid.
     796                 :            :          * Bottom line is that relacl *cannot* be retrieved from the relcache. Get
     797                 :            :          * it from the syscache if you need it.  The same goes for the original
     798                 :            :          * form of reloptions (however, we do store the parsed form of reloptions
     799                 :            :          * in rd_options).
     800                 :            :          */
     801                 :     881176 :         relationForm = (Form_pg_class) palloc(CLASS_TUPLE_SIZE);
     802                 :            : 
     803                 :     881176 :         memcpy(relationForm, relp, CLASS_TUPLE_SIZE);
     804                 :            : 
     805                 :            :         /* initialize relation tuple form */
     806                 :     881176 :         relation->rd_rel = relationForm;
     807                 :            : 
     808                 :            :         /*
     809                 :            :          * This part MUST be remain as a fetch on demand, otherwise you end up
     810                 :            :          * needing it to open pg_class and then relation_open does infinite recursion...
     811                 :            :          */
     812                 :     881176 :         relation->rd_segfile0_relationnodeinfo.isPresent = false;
     813                 :     881176 :         relation->rd_segfile0_relationnodeinfo.tidAllowedToBeZero = false;
     814                 :            : 
     815                 :            :         /* and allocate attribute tuple form storage */
     816                 :     881176 :         relation->rd_att = CreateTemplateTupleDesc(relationForm->relnatts,
     817                 :     881176 :                                                                                            relationForm->relhasoids);
     818                 :            :         /* which we mark as a reference-counted tupdesc */
     819                 :     881176 :         relation->rd_att->tdrefcount = 1;
     820                 :            : 
     821                 :     881176 :         MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcxt);
     822                 :            : 
     823                 :     881176 :         return relation;
     824                 :            : }
     825                 :            : 
     826                 :            : /*
     827                 :            :  * RelationParseRelOptions
     828                 :            :  *              Convert pg_class.reloptions into pre-parsed rd_options
     829                 :            :  *
     830                 :            :  * tuple is the real pg_class tuple (not rd_rel!) for relation
     831                 :            :  *
     832                 :            :  * Note: rd_rel and (if an index) rd_am must be valid already
     833                 :            :  */
     834                 :            : static void
     835                 :            : RelationParseRelOptions(Relation relation, HeapTuple tuple)
     836                 :            : {
     837                 :            :         Datum           datum;
     838                 :            :         bool            isnull;
     839                 :            :         bytea      *options;
     840                 :            : 
     841                 :    1106424 :         relation->rd_options = NULL;
     842                 :            : 
     843                 :            :         /* Fall out if relkind should not have options */
     844   [ -  -  -  -  :    1106424 :         switch (relation->rd_rel->relkind)
                -  +  + ]
     845                 :            :         {
     846                 :            :                 case RELKIND_RELATION:
     847                 :            :                 case RELKIND_TOASTVALUE:
     848                 :            :                 case RELKIND_AOSEGMENTS:
     849                 :            :                 case RELKIND_AOBLOCKDIR:
     850                 :            :                 case RELKIND_AOVISIMAP:
     851                 :            :                 case RELKIND_INDEX:
     852                 :    1102351 :                         break;

     854                 :       4073 :                         return;
     855                 :            :         }
     856                 :            : 
     857                 :            :         /*
     858                 :            :          * Fetch reloptions from tuple; have to use a hardwired descriptor because

     863                 :            :                                                 Anum_pg_class_reloptions,
     864                 :    1102351 :                                                 GetPgClassDescriptor(),

     866         [ +  + ]:    1102351 :         if (isnull)
     867                 :     966356 :                 return;
     868                 :            : 
     869                 :            :         /* Parse into appropriate format; don't error out here */
     870   [ -  -  -  -  :     135995 :         switch (relation->rd_rel->relkind)
             -  +  +  - ]
     871                 :            :         {
     872                 :            :                 case RELKIND_RELATION:
     873                 :            :                 case RELKIND_TOASTVALUE:
     874                 :            :                 case RELKIND_AOSEGMENTS:
     875                 :            :                 case RELKIND_AOBLOCKDIR:
     876                 :            :                 case RELKIND_AOVISIMAP:
     877                 :            :                 case RELKIND_UNCATALOGED:
     878                 :     135500 :                         options = heap_reloptions(relation->rd_rel->relkind, datum,
     879                 :            :                                                                           false);
     880                 :     135500 :                         break;
     881                 :            :                 case RELKIND_INDEX:
     882                 :        495 :                         options = index_reloptions(relation->rd_am->amoptions, datum,
     883                 :            :                                                                            false);
     884                 :        495 :                         break;
     885                 :            :                 default:
     886         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         Assert(false);          /* can't get here */
     887                 :          0 :                         options = NULL;         /* keep compiler quiet */
     888                 :          0 :                         break;
     889                 :            :         }
     890                 :            : 
     891                 :            :         /*
     892                 :            :          * Copy parsed data into CacheMemoryContext.  To guard against the
     893                 :            :          * possibility of leaks in the reloptions code, we want to do the actual
     894                 :            :          * parsing in the caller's memory context and copy the results into
     895                 :            :          * CacheMemoryContext after the fact.
     896                 :            :          */
     897         [ +  - ]:     135995 :         if (options)
     898                 :            :         {
     899                 :     135995 :                 relation->rd_options = MemoryContextAlloc(CacheMemoryContext,
     900                 :            :                                                                                                   VARSIZE(options));
     901                 :     135995 :                 memcpy(relation->rd_options, options, VARSIZE(options));
     902                 :     135995 :                 pfree(options);

     906                 :            : /*

     911                 :            :  */

     924                 :            :         /* copy some fields from pg_class row to rd_att */

     928                 :            :

     933                 :            :         /*

     935                 :            :          * (Eliminating system attribute rows at the index level is lots faster

     939                 :            :                                 Anum_pg_attribute_attrelid,
     940                 :            :                                 BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,

     942                 :     881176 :         ScanKeyInit(&skey[1],

     950                 :            :          * without a pg_internal.init file).
     951                 :            :          */

     953                 :     881176 :         pg_attribute_scan = systable_beginscan(pg_attribute_desc,

     955                 :     881176 :                                                                                    criticalRelcachesBuilt,
     956                 :            :                                                                                    SnapshotNow,
     957                 :     881176 :                                                                                    2, skey);
     958                 :            : 
     959                 :            :         /*
     960                 :            :          * add attribute data to relation->rd_att
     961                 :            :          */

     963                 :            : 
     964         [ +  + ]:    5394688 :         while (HeapTupleIsValid(pg_attribute_tuple = systable_getnext(pg_attribute_scan)))
     965                 :            :         {

     967                 :            : 
     968                 :    5394623 :                 attp = (Form_pg_attribute) GETSTRUCT(pg_attribute_tuple);
     969                 :            : 
     970 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:    5394623 :                 if (attp->attnum <= 0 ||
     971                 :    5394623 :                         attp->attnum > relation->rd_rel->relnatts)

     974                 :            :

     987                 :      38380 :                                         MemoryContextAllocZero(CacheMemoryContext,
     988                 :            :                                                                                    relation->rd_rel->relnatts *
     989                 :            :                                                                                    sizeof(AttrDefault));
     990                 :     110891 :                         attrdef[ndef].adnum = attp->attnum;
     991                 :     110891 :                         attrdef[ndef].adbin = NULL;

     993                 :     110891 :                 }

     997                 :            :         }

    1000                 :            :          * end the scan and close the attribute relation
    1001                 :            :          */
    1002                 :     881176 :         systable_endscan(pg_attribute_scan);
    1003                 :     881176 :         heap_close(pg_attribute_desc, AccessShareLock);
    1004                 :            : 
    1005         [ -  + ]:     881176 :         if (need != 0)
    1006                 :          0 :                 elog(ERROR, "catalog is missing %d attribute(s) for relid %u",
    1007                 :            :                          need, RelationGetRelid(relation));
    1008                 :            : 
    1009                 :            :         /*
    1010                 :            :          * The attcacheoff values we read from pg_attribute should all be -1
    1011                 :            :          * ("unknown").  Verify this if assert checking is on.        They will be

    1016                 :            :                 int                     i;
    1017                 :            :

    1069                 :            : /*
    1070                 :            :  *              RelationBuildRuleLock
    1071                 :            :  *
    1072                 :            :  *              Form the relation's rewrite rules from information in
    1073                 :            :  *              the pg_rewrite system catalog.
    1074                 :            :  *
    1075                 :            :  * Note: The rule parsetrees are potentially very complex node structures.
    1076                 :            :  * To allow these trees to be freed when the relcache entry is flushed,
    1077                 :            :  * we make a private memory context to hold the RuleLock information for
    1078                 :            :  * each relcache entry that has associated rules.  The context is used
    1079                 :            :  * just for rule info, not for any other subsidiary data of the relcache
    1080                 :            :  * entry, because that keeps the update logic in RelationClearRelation()
    1081                 :            :  * manageable.  The other subsidiary data structures are simple enough
    1082                 :            :  * to be easy to free explicitly, anyway.
    1083                 :            :  */
    1084                 :            : static void
    1085                 :            : RelationBuildRuleLock(Relation relation)
    1086                 :            : {
    1087                 :            :         MemoryContext rulescxt;
    1088                 :            :         MemoryContext oldcxt;
    1089                 :            :         HeapTuple       rewrite_tuple;
    1090                 :            :         Relation        rewrite_desc;
    1091                 :            :         TupleDesc       rewrite_tupdesc;
    1092                 :            :         SysScanDesc rewrite_scan;
    1093                 :            :         ScanKeyData key;
    1094                 :            :         RuleLock   *rulelock;
    1095                 :            :         int                     numlocks;
    1096                 :            :         RewriteRule **rules;
    1097                 :            :         int                     maxlocks;
    1098                 :            : 
    1099                 :            :         /*
    1100                 :            :          * Make the private context.  Parameters are set on the assumption that
    1101                 :            :          * it'll probably not contain much data.
    1102                 :            :          */
    1103                 :       1489 :         rulescxt = AllocSetContextCreate(CacheMemoryContext,
    1104                 :       1489 :                                                                          RelationGetRelationName(relation),
    1105                 :            :                                                                          ALLOCSET_SMALL_MINSIZE,
    1106                 :            :                                                                          ALLOCSET_SMALL_INITSIZE,
    1107                 :            :                                                                          ALLOCSET_SMALL_MAXSIZE);
    1108                 :       1489 :         relation->rd_rulescxt = rulescxt;
    1109                 :            : 
    1110                 :            :         /*
    1111                 :            :          * allocate an array to hold the rewrite rules (the array is extended if
    1112                 :            :          * necessary)
    1113                 :            :          */
    1114                 :       1489 :         maxlocks = 4;
    1115                 :       1489 :         rules = (RewriteRule **)
    1116                 :       1489 :                 MemoryContextAlloc(rulescxt, sizeof(RewriteRule *) * maxlocks);
    1117                 :       1489 :         numlocks = 0;
    1118                 :            : 
    1119                 :            :         /*
    1120                 :            :          * form a scan key
    1121                 :            :          */
    1122                 :       1489 :         ScanKeyInit(&key,
    1123                 :            :                                 Anum_pg_rewrite_ev_class,
    1124                 :            :                                 BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
    1125                 :       1489 :                                 ObjectIdGetDatum(RelationGetRelid(relation)));
    1126                 :            : 
    1127                 :            :         /*
    1128                 :            :          * open pg_rewrite and begin a scan
    1129                 :            :          *
    1130                 :            :          * Note: since we scan the rules using RewriteRelRulenameIndexId, we will
    1131                 :            :          * be reading the rules in name order, except possibly during
    1132                 :            :          * emergency-recovery operations (ie, IgnoreSystemIndexes). This in turn
    1133                 :            :          * ensures that rules will be fired in name order.
    1134                 :            :          */
    1135                 :       1489 :         rewrite_desc = heap_open(RewriteRelationId, AccessShareLock);
    1136                 :       1489 :         rewrite_tupdesc = RelationGetDescr(rewrite_desc);
    1137                 :            : 
    1138                 :       1489 :         rewrite_scan = systable_beginscan(rewrite_desc,
    1139                 :            :                                                                           RewriteRelRulenameIndexId,
    1140                 :            :                                                                           true, SnapshotNow,
    1141                 :            :                                                                           1, &key);
    1142                 :            : 
    1143         [ +  + ]:       3085 :         while (HeapTupleIsValid(rewrite_tuple = systable_getnext(rewrite_scan)))
    1144                 :            :         {
    1145                 :       1596 :                 Form_pg_rewrite rewrite_form = (Form_pg_rewrite) GETSTRUCT(rewrite_tuple);
    1146                 :            :                 bool            isnull;
    1147                 :            :                 Datum           rule_datum;
    1148                 :            :                 char       *rule_str;
    1149                 :            :                 RewriteRule *rule;
    1150                 :            : 
    1151                 :       1596 :                 rule = (RewriteRule *) MemoryContextAlloc(rulescxt,
    1152                 :            :                                                                                                   sizeof(RewriteRule));
    1153                 :            : 
    1154         [ +  - ]:       1596 :                 rule->ruleId = HeapTupleGetOid(rewrite_tuple);
    1155                 :            : 
    1156                 :       1596 :                 rule->event = rewrite_form->ev_type - '0';
    1157                 :       1596 :                 rule->attrno = rewrite_form->ev_attr;
    1158                 :       1596 :                 rule->enabled = rewrite_form->ev_enabled;
    1159                 :       1596 :                 rule->isInstead = rewrite_form->is_instead;
    1160                 :            : 
    1161                 :            :                 /*
    1162                 :            :                  * Must use heap_getattr to fetch ev_action and ev_qual.  Also, the
    1163                 :            :                  * rule strings are often large enough to be toasted.  To avoid
    1164                 :            :                  * leaking memory in the caller's context, do the detoasting here so
    1165                 :            :                  * we can free the detoasted version.
    1166                 :            :                  */
    1167                 :       1596 :                 rule_datum = heap_getattr(rewrite_tuple,
    1168                 :            :                                                                   Anum_pg_rewrite_ev_action,
    1169                 :       1596 :                                                                   rewrite_tupdesc,
    1170                 :            :                                                                   &isnull);
    1171 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:       1596 :                 Assert(!isnull);
    1172                 :       1596 :                 rule_str = TextDatumGetCString(rule_datum);
    1173                 :       1596 :                 oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(rulescxt);
    1174                 :       1596 :                 rule->actions = (List *) stringToNode(rule_str);
    1175                 :       1596 :                 MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcxt);
    1176                 :       1596 :                 pfree(rule_str);
    1177                 :            : 
    1178                 :       1596 :                 rule_datum = heap_getattr(rewrite_tuple,
    1179                 :            :                                                                   Anum_pg_rewrite_ev_qual,
    1180                 :       1596 :                                                                   rewrite_tupdesc,
    1181                 :            :                                                                   &isnull);
    1182 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:       1596 :                 Assert(!isnull);
    1183                 :       1596 :                 rule_str = TextDatumGetCString(rule_datum);
    1184                 :       1596 :                 oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(rulescxt);
    1185                 :       1596 :                 rule->qual = (Node *) stringToNode(rule_str);
    1186                 :       1596 :                 MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcxt);
    1187                 :       1596 :                 pfree(rule_str);
    1188                 :            : 
    1189                 :            :                 /*
    1190                 :            :                  * We want the rule's table references to be checked as though by the
    1191                 :            :                  * table owner, not the user referencing the rule.      Therefore, scan
    1192                 :            :                  * through the rule's actions and set the checkAsUser field on all
    1193                 :            :                  * rtable entries.      We have to look at the qual as well, in case it
    1194                 :            :                  * contains sublinks.
    1195                 :            :                  *
    1196                 :            :                  * The reason for doing this when the rule is loaded, rather than when
    1197                 :            :                  * it is stored, is that otherwise ALTER TABLE OWNER would have to
    1198                 :            :                  * grovel through stored rules to update checkAsUser fields. Scanning
    1199                 :            :                  * the rule tree during load is relatively cheap (compared to
    1200                 :            :                  * constructing it in the first place), so we do it here.
    1201                 :            :                  */
    1202                 :       1596 :                 setRuleCheckAsUser((Node *) rule->actions, relation->rd_rel->relowner);
    1203                 :       1596 :                 setRuleCheckAsUser(rule->qual, relation->rd_rel->relowner);
    1204                 :            : 
    1205         [ -  + ]:       1596 :                 if (numlocks >= maxlocks)
    1206                 :            :                 {
    1207                 :          0 :                         maxlocks *= 2;
    1208                 :          0 :                         rules = (RewriteRule **)
    1209                 :          0 :                                 repalloc(rules, sizeof(RewriteRule *) * maxlocks);
    1210                 :          0 :                 }
    1211                 :       1596 :                 rules[numlocks++] = rule;
    1212                 :            :         }
    1213                 :            : 
    1214                 :            :         /*
    1215                 :            :          * end the scan and close the attribute relation
    1216                 :            :          */
    1217                 :       1489 :         systable_endscan(rewrite_scan);
    1218                 :       1489 :         heap_close(rewrite_desc, AccessShareLock);
    1219                 :            : 
    1220                 :            :         /*
    1221                 :            :          * form a RuleLock and insert into relation
    1222                 :            :          */
    1223                 :       1489 :         rulelock = (RuleLock *) MemoryContextAlloc(rulescxt, sizeof(RuleLock));
    1224                 :       1489 :         rulelock->numLocks = numlocks;
    1225                 :       1489 :         rulelock->rules = rules;
    1226                 :            : 
    1227                 :       1489 :         relation->rd_rules = rulelock;
    1228                 :       1489 : }
    1229                 :            : 
    1230                 :            : /*
    1231                 :            :  *              equalRuleLocks
    1232                 :            :  *
    1233                 :            :  *              Determine whether two RuleLocks are equivalent
    1234                 :            :  *
    1235                 :            :  *              Probably this should be in the rules code someplace...
    1236                 :            :  */
    1237                 :            : static bool
    1238                 :            : equalRuleLocks(RuleLock *rlock1, RuleLock *rlock2)
    1239                 :            : {
    1240                 :            :         int                     i;
    1241                 :            : 
    1242                 :            :         /*
    1243                 :            :          * As of 7.3 we assume the rule ordering is repeatable, because
    1244                 :            :          * RelationBuildRuleLock should read 'em in a consistent order.  So just
    1245                 :            :          * compare corresponding slots.
    1246                 :            :          */
    1247         [ +  + ]:     333960 :         if (rlock1 != NULL)
    1248                 :            :         {
    1249         [ +  + ]:         20 :                 if (rlock2 == NULL)
    1250                 :          8 :                         return false;
    1251         [ -  + ]:         12 :                 if (rlock1->numLocks != rlock2->numLocks)
    1252                 :          0 :                         return false;
    1253         [ +  + ]:         32 :                 for (i = 0; i < rlock1->numLocks; i++)
    1254                 :            :                 {
    1255                 :         20 :                         RewriteRule *rule1 = rlock1->rules[i];
    1256                 :         20 :                         RewriteRule *rule2 = rlock2->rules[i];
    1257                 :            : 
    1258         [ -  + ]:         20 :                         if (rule1->ruleId != rule2->ruleId)
    1259                 :          0 :                                 return false;
    1260         [ -  + ]:         20 :                         if (rule1->event != rule2->event)
    1261                 :          0 :                                 return false;
    1262         [ -  + ]:         20 :                         if (rule1->attrno != rule2->attrno)
    1263                 :          0 :                                 return false;
    1264         [ -  + ]:         20 :                         if (rule1->enabled != rule2->enabled)
    1265                 :          0 :                                 return false;
    1266         [ -  + ]:         20 :                         if (rule1->isInstead != rule2->isInstead)
    1267                 :          0 :                                 return false;
    1268         [ -  + ]:         20 :                         if (!equal(rule1->qual, rule2->qual))
    1269                 :          0 :                                 return false;
    1270         [ -  + ]:         20 :                         if (!equal(rule1->actions, rule2->actions))
    1271                 :          0 :                                 return false;
    1272                 :         20 :                 }
    1273                 :         12 :         }
    1274         [ +  + ]:     333940 :         else if (rlock2 != NULL)
    1275                 :        535 :                 return false;
    1276                 :     333417 :         return true;
    1277                 :     333960 : }
    1278                 :            : 
    1279                 :            : 
    1280                 :            : /*
    1281                 :            :  *              RelationBuildDesc
    1282                 :            :  *
    1283                 :            :  *              Build a relation descriptor.  The caller must hold at least
    1284                 :            :  *              AccessShareLock on the target relid.
    1285                 :            :  *
    1286                 :            :  *              The new descriptor is inserted into the hash table if insertIt is true.
    1287                 :            :  *
    1288                 :            :  *              Returns NULL if no pg_class row could be found for the given relid
    1289                 :            :  *              (suggesting we are trying to access a just-deleted relation).
    1290                 :            :  *              Any other error is reported via elog.
    1291                 :            :  */
    1292                 :            : static Relation
    1293                 :            : RelationBuildDesc(Oid targetRelId, bool insertIt)
    1294                 :            : {
    1295                 :            :         Relation        relation;
    1296                 :            :         Oid                     relid;
    1297                 :            :         Relation    pg_class_relation;
    1298                 :            :         HeapTuple       pg_class_tuple;
    1299                 :            :         Form_pg_class relp;
    1300                 :            : 
    1301                 :            :         /*
    1302                 :            :          * find the tuple in pg_class corresponding to the given relation id
    1303                 :            :          */
    1304                 :     881176 :         pg_class_tuple = ScanPgRelation(targetRelId, true, &pg_class_relation);
    1305                 :            : 
    1306                 :            :         /*
    1307                 :            :          * if no such tuple exists, return NULL
    1308                 :            :          */
    1309         [ -  + ]:     881176 :         if (!HeapTupleIsValid(pg_class_tuple))
    1310                 :          0 :                 return NULL;
    1311                 :            : 
    1312                 :            :         /*
    1313                 :            :          * get information from the pg_class_tuple
    1314                 :            :          */
    1315         [ +  - ]:     881176 :         relid = HeapTupleGetOid(pg_class_tuple);
    1316                 :     881176 :         relp = (Form_pg_class) GETSTRUCT(pg_class_tuple);
    1317                 :     881176 :         heap_close(pg_class_relation, AccessShareLock);
    1318                 :            : 
    1319                 :            :         /*
    1320                 :            :          * allocate storage for the relation descriptor, and copy pg_class_tuple
    1321                 :            :          * to relation->rd_rel and new fields into relation->rd_newfields.
    1322                 :            :          */
    1323                 :     881176 :         relation = AllocateRelationDesc(relp);
    1324                 :            : 
    1325                 :            :         /*
    1326                 :            :          * initialize the relation's relation id (relation->rd_id)
    1327                 :            :          */
    1328                 :     881176 :         RelationGetRelid(relation) = relid;
    1329                 :            : 
    1330                 :            :         /*
    1331                 :            :          * normal relations are not nailed into the cache; nor can a pre-existing
    1332                 :            :          * relation be new.  It could be temp though.  (Actually, it could be new
    1333                 :            :          * too, but it's okay to forget that fact if forced to flush the entry.)
    1334                 :            :          */
    1335                 :     881176 :         relation->rd_refcnt = 0;
    1336                 :     881176 :         relation->rd_isnailed = false;

    1598                 :            : /*
    1599                 :            :  * IndexSupportInitialize
    1600                 :            :  *              Initializes an index's cached opclass information,
    1601                 :            :  *              given the index's pg_index.indclass entry.
    1602                 :            :  *
    1603                 :            :  * Data is returned into *indexOperator, *indexSupport, *opFamily, and
    1604                 :            :  * *opcInType, which are arrays allocated by the caller.
    1605                 :            :  *
    1606                 :            :  * The caller also passes maxStrategyNumber, maxSupportNumber, and
    1607                 :            :  * maxAttributeNumber, since these indicate the size of the arrays
    1608                 :            :  * it has allocated --- but in practice these numbers must always match
    1609                 :            :  * those obtainable from the system catalog entries for the index and
    1610                 :            :  * access method.

    1649                 :            : 
    1650                 :            : /*
    1651                 :            :  * LookupOpclassInfo
    1652                 :            :  *
    1653                 :            :  * This routine maintains a per-opclass cache of the information needed
    1654                 :            :  * by IndexSupportInitialize().  This is more efficient than relying on
    1655                 :            :  * the catalog cache, because we can load all the info about a particular
    1656                 :            :  * opclass in a single indexscan of pg_amproc or pg_amop.
    1657                 :            :  *
    1658                 :            :  * The information from pg_am about expected range of strategy and support
    1659                 :            :  * numbers is passed in, rather than being looked up, mainly because the
    1660                 :            :  * caller will have it already.
    1661                 :            :  *
    1662                 :            :  * Note there is no provision for flushing the cache.  This is OK at the
    1663                 :            :  * moment because there is no way to ALTER any interesting properties of an
    1664                 :            :  * existing opclass --- all you can do is drop it, which will result in
    1665                 :            :  * a useless but harmless dead entry in the cache.  To support altering
    1666                 :            :  * opclass membership (not the same as opfamily membership!), we'd need to
    1667                 :            :  * be able to flush this cache as well as the contents of relcache entries
    1668                 :            :  * for indexes.
    1669                 :            :  */
    1670                 :            : static OpClassCacheEnt *
    1671                 :            : LookupOpclassInfo(Oid operatorClassOid,
    1672                 :            :                                   StrategyNumber numStrats,
    1673                 :            :                                   StrategyNumber numSupport)
    1674                 :            : {
    1675                 :            :         OpClassCacheEnt *opcentry;
    1676                 :            :         bool            found;
    1677                 :            :         Relation        rel;
    1678                 :            :         SysScanDesc scan;
    1679                 :            :         ScanKeyData skey[3];
    1680                 :            :         HeapTuple       htup;
    1681                 :            :         bool            indexOK;
    1682                 :            : 
    1683         [ +  + ]:     354325 :         if (OpClassCache == NULL)
    1684                 :            :         {
    1685                 :            :                 /* First time through: initialize the opclass cache */
    1686                 :            :                 HASHCTL         ctl;
    1687                 :            : 
    1688         [ -  + ]:       8584 :                 if (!CacheMemoryContext)
    1689                 :          0 :                         CreateCacheMemoryContext();
    1690                 :            : 
    1691 [ +  - ][ +  - ]:     120176 :                 MemSet(&ctl, 0, sizeof(ctl));
         [ +  - ][ +  - ]
                 [ +  + ]
    1692                 :       8584 :                 ctl.keysize = sizeof(Oid);
    1693                 :       8584 :                 ctl.entrysize = sizeof(OpClassCacheEnt);
    1694                 :       8584 :                 ctl.hash = oid_hash;
    1695                 :       8584 :                 OpClassCache = hash_create("Operator class cache", 64,
    1696                 :            :                                                                    &ctl, HASH_ELEM | HASH_FUNCTION);
    1697                 :       8584 :         }
    1698                 :            : 
    1699                 :     354325 :         opcentry = (OpClassCacheEnt *) hash_search(OpClassCache,
    1700                 :     354325 :                                                                                            (void *) &operatorClassOid,
    1701                 :            :                                                                                            HASH_ENTER, &found);
    1702                 :            : 
    1703         [ +  + ]:     354325 :         if (!found)
    1704                 :            :         {
    1705                 :            :                 /* Need to allocate memory for new entry */
    1706                 :      19841 :                 opcentry->valid = false;     /* until known OK */
    1707                 :      19841 :                 opcentry->numStrats = numStrats;
    1708                 :      19841 :                 opcentry->numSupport = numSupport;
    1709                 :            : 
    1710         [ +  + ]:      19841 :                 if (numStrats > 0)
    1711                 :      19761 :                         opcentry->operatorOids = (Oid *)
    1712                 :      19761 :                                 MemoryContextAllocZero(CacheMemoryContext,
    1713                 :            :                                                                            numStrats * sizeof(Oid));
    1714                 :            :                 else
    1715                 :         80 :                         opcentry->operatorOids = NULL;
    1716                 :            : 
    1717         [ +  - ]:      19841 :                 if (numSupport > 0)
    1718                 :      19841 :                         opcentry->supportProcs = (RegProcedure *)
    1719                 :      19841 :                                 MemoryContextAllocZero(CacheMemoryContext,
    1720                 :            :                                                                            numSupport * sizeof(RegProcedure));
    1721                 :            :                 else
    1722                 :          0 :                         opcentry->supportProcs = NULL;
    1723                 :      19841 :         }
    1724                 :            :         else
    1725                 :            :         {
    1726 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:     334484 :                 Assert(numStrats == opcentry->numStrats);
    1727 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:     334484 :                 Assert(numSupport == opcentry->numSupport);
    1728                 :            :         }
    1729                 :            : 
    1730                 :            :         /*
    1731                 :            :          * When testing for cache-flush hazards, we intentionally disable the
    1732                 :            :          * operator class cache and force reloading of the info on each call.
    1733                 :            :          * This is helpful because we want to test the case where a cache flush
    1734                 :            :          * occurs while we are loading the info, and it's very hard to provoke
    1735                 :            :          * that if this happens only once per opclass per backend.
    1736                 :            :          */
    1737                 :            : #if defined(CLOBBER_CACHE_ALWAYS)
    1738                 :            :         opcentry->valid = false;
    1739                 :            : #endif
    1740                 :            : 
    1741         [ +  + ]:     354325 :         if (opcentry->valid)
    1742                 :     334484 :                 return opcentry;
    1743                 :            : 
    1744                 :            :         /*
    1745                 :            :          * Need to fill in new entry.
    1746                 :            :          *
    1747                 :            :          * To avoid infinite recursion during startup, force heap scans if we're
    1748                 :            :          * looking up info for the opclasses used by the indexes we would like to
    1749                 :            :          * reference here.
    1750                 :            :          */
    1751         [ +  + ]:      19841 :         indexOK = criticalRelcachesBuilt ||
    1752         [ +  + ]:        384 :                 (operatorClassOid != OID_BTREE_OPS_OID &&
    1753                 :        256 :                  operatorClassOid != INT2_BTREE_OPS_OID);
    1754                 :            : 
    1755                 :            :         /*
    1756                 :            :          * We have to fetch the pg_opclass row to determine its opfamily and
    1757                 :            :          * opcintype, which are needed to look up the operators and functions.
    1758                 :            :          * It'd be convenient to use the syscache here, but that probably doesn't
    1759                 :            :          * work while bootstrapping.
    1760                 :            :          */
    1761                 :      19841 :         ScanKeyInit(&skey[0],
    1762                 :            :                                 ObjectIdAttributeNumber,

    1764                 :      19841 :                                 ObjectIdGetDatum(operatorClassOid));
    1765                 :      19841 :         rel = heap_open(OperatorClassRelationId, AccessShareLock);
    1766                 :      19841 :         scan = systable_beginscan(rel, OpclassOidIndexId, indexOK,
    1767                 :      19841 :                                                           SnapshotNow, 1, skey);
    1768                 :            : 
    1769         [ +  - ]:      19841 :         if (HeapTupleIsValid(htup = systable_getnext(scan)))
    1770                 :            :         {

    1772                 :            : 
    1773                 :      19841 :                 opcentry->opcfamily = opclassform->opcfamily;
