    1864                 :            : 
    1865                 :            : /*
    1866                 :            :  *              formrdesc
    1867                 :            :  *
    1868                 :            :  *              This is a special cut-down version of RelationBuildDesc(),
    1869                 :            :  *              used while initializing the relcache.
    1870                 :            :  *              The relation descriptor is built just from the supplied parameters,
    1871                 :            :  *              without actually looking at any system table entries.  We cheat
    1872                 :            :  *              quite a lot since we only need to work for a few basic system
    1873                 :            :  *              catalogs.
    1874                 :            :  *
    1875                 :            :  * formrdesc is currently used for: pg_database, pg_authid, pg_auth_members,
    1876                 :            :  * pg_class, pg_attribute, pg_proc, and pg_type
    1877                 :            :  * (see RelationCacheInitializePhase2/3).
    1878                 :            :  *
    1879                 :            :  * Note that these catalogs can't have constraints (except attnotnull),
    1880                 :            :  * default values, rules, or triggers, since we don't cope with any of that.
    1881                 :            :  * (Well, actually, this only matters for properties that need to be valid
    1882                 :            :  * during bootstrap or before RelationCacheInitializePhase3 runs, and none of
    1883                 :            :  * these properties matter then...)
    1884                 :            :  *
    1885                 :            :  * NOTE: we assume we are already switched into CacheMemoryContext.
    1886                 :            :  */
    1887                 :            : static void
    1888                 :            : formrdesc(const char *relationName, Oid relationReltype,
    1889                 :            :                   bool isshared, bool hasoids,
    1890                 :            :                   int natts, const FormData_pg_attribute *attrs)
    1891                 :            : {
    1892                 :            :         Relation        relation;
    1893                 :            :         int                     i;
    1894                 :            :         bool            has_not_null;
    1895                 :            : 
    1896                 :            :         /*
    1897                 :            :          * allocate new relation desc, clear all fields of reldesc
    1898                 :            :          */
    1899                 :        578 :         relation = (Relation) palloc0(sizeof(RelationData));
    1900                 :        578 :         relation->rd_targblock = InvalidBlockNumber;
    1901                 :            : 
    1902                 :            :         /* make sure relation is marked as having no open file yet */
    1903                 :        578 :         relation->rd_smgr = NULL;
    1904                 :            : 
    1905                 :            :         /*
    1906                 :            :          * initialize reference count: 1 because it is nailed in cache
    1907                 :            :          */
    1908                 :        578 :         relation->rd_refcnt = 1;
    1909                 :            : 
    1910                 :            :         /*
    1911                 :            :          * all entries built with this routine are nailed-in-cache; none are for
    1912                 :            :          * new or temp relations.
    1913                 :            :          */
    1914                 :        578 :         relation->rd_isnailed = true;
    1915                 :        578 :         relation->rd_createSubid = InvalidSubTransactionId;
    1916                 :        578 :         relation->rd_newRelfilenodeSubid = InvalidSubTransactionId;
    1917                 :        578 :         relation->rd_istemp = false;
    1918                 :        578 :         relation->rd_issyscat = (strncmp(relationName, "pg_", 3) == 0);    /* GP */
    1919                 :        578 :     relation->rd_isLocalBuf = false;    /*CDB*/
    1920                 :            : 
    1921                 :            :         /*
    1922                 :            :          * initialize relation tuple form
    1923                 :            :          *
    1924                 :            :          * The data we insert here is pretty incomplete/bogus, but it'll serve to
    1925                 :            :          * get us launched.  RelationCacheInitializePhase2() will read the real
    1926                 :            :          * data from pg_class and replace what we've done here.  Note in particular
    1927                 :            :          * that relowner is left as zero; this cues RelationCacheInitializePhase2
    1928                 :            :          * that the real data isn't there yet.
    1929                 :            :          */
    1930                 :        578 :         relation->rd_rel = (Form_pg_class) palloc0(CLASS_TUPLE_SIZE);
    1931                 :            : 
    1932                 :        578 :         namestrcpy(&relation->rd_rel->relname, relationName);
    1933                 :        578 :         relation->rd_rel->relnamespace = PG_CATALOG_NAMESPACE;
    1934                 :        578 :         relation->rd_rel->reltype = relationReltype;
    1935                 :            : 
    1936                 :            :         /*
    1937                 :            :          * It's important to distinguish between shared and non-shared relations,
    1938                 :            :          * even at bootstrap time, to make sure we know where they are stored.
    1939                 :            :          */
    1940                 :        578 :         relation->rd_rel->relisshared = isshared;
    1941         [ +  + ]:        578 :         if (isshared)
    1942                 :         66 :                 relation->rd_rel->reltablespace = GLOBALTABLESPACE_OID;
    1943                 :            : 
    1944                 :        578 :         relation->rd_rel->relpages = 0;
    1945                 :        578 :         relation->rd_rel->reltuples = 0;
    1946                 :        578 :         relation->rd_rel->relkind = RELKIND_RELATION;
    1947                 :        578 :         relation->rd_rel->relstorage = RELSTORAGE_HEAP;
    1948                 :        578 :         relation->rd_rel->relhasoids = hasoids;
    1949                 :        578 :         relation->rd_rel->relnatts = (int16) natts;
    1950                 :            : 
    1951                 :            :         /*
    1952                 :            :          * Physical file-system information.
    1953                 :            :          */
    1954                 :        578 :         relation->rd_segfile0_relationnodeinfo.isPresent = false;
    1955                 :        578 :         relation->rd_segfile0_relationnodeinfo.tidAllowedToBeZero = false;
    1956                 :            :         
    1957                 :            :         /*
    1958                 :            :          * initialize attribute tuple form
    1959                 :            :          *
    1960                 :            :          * Unlike the case with the relation tuple, this data had better be right
    1961                 :            :          * because it will never be replaced.  The input values must be correctly
    1962                 :            :          * defined by macros in src/include/catalog/ headers.
    1963                 :            :          */
    1964                 :        578 :         relation->rd_att = CreateTemplateTupleDesc(natts, hasoids);
    1965                 :        578 :         relation->rd_att->tdrefcount = 1; /* mark as refcounted */
    1966                 :            : 
    1967                 :        578 :         relation->rd_att->tdtypeid = relationReltype;
    1968                 :        578 :         relation->rd_att->tdtypmod = -1;  /* unnecessary, but... */
    1969                 :            : 
    1970                 :            :         /*
    1971                 :            :          * initialize tuple desc info
    1972                 :            :          */
    1973                 :        578 :         has_not_null = false;
    1974         [ +  + ]:      13720 :         for (i = 0; i < natts; i++)
    1975                 :            :         {
    1976                 :      13142 :                 memcpy(relation->rd_att->attrs[i],
    1977                 :            :                            &attrs[i],
    1978                 :            :                            ATTRIBUTE_FIXED_PART_SIZE);
    1979                 :      13142 :                 has_not_null |= attrs[i].attnotnull;
    1980                 :            :                 /* make sure attcacheoff is valid */
    1981                 :      13142 :                 relation->rd_att->attrs[i]->attcacheoff = -1;
    1982                 :      13142 :         }
    1983                 :            : 
    1984                 :            :         /* initialize first attribute's attcacheoff, cf RelationBuildTupleDesc */
    1985                 :        578 :         relation->rd_att->attrs[0]->attcacheoff = 0;
    1986                 :            : 
    1987                 :            :         /* mark not-null status */
    1988         [ +  - ]:        578 :         if (has_not_null)
    1989                 :            :         {
    1990                 :        578 :                 TupleConstr *constr = (TupleConstr *) palloc0(sizeof(TupleConstr));
    1991                 :            : 
    1992                 :        578 :                 constr->has_not_null = true;
    1993                 :        578 :                 relation->rd_att->constr = constr;
    1994                 :        578 :         }
    1995                 :            : 
    1996                 :            :         /*
    1997                 :            :          * initialize relation id from info in att array (my, this is ugly)
    1998                 :            :          */
    1999                 :        578 :         RelationGetRelid(relation) = relation->rd_att->attrs[0]->attrelid;
    2000                 :        578 :         relation->rd_rel->relfilenode = RelationGetRelid(relation);
    2001                 :            : 
    2002                 :            :         /*
    2003                 :            :          * initialize the relation lock manager information
    2004                 :            :          */
    2005                 :        578 :         RelationInitLockInfo(relation);         /* see lmgr.c */
    2006                 :            : 
    2007                 :            :         /*
    2008                 :            :          * initialize physical addressing information for the relation
    2009                 :            :          */
    2010                 :        578 :         RelationInitPhysicalAddr(relation);
    2011                 :            : 
    2012                 :            :         /*
    2013                 :            :          * initialize the rel-has-index flag, using hardwired knowledge
    2014                 :            :          */
    2015         [ -  + ]:        578 :         if (IsBootstrapProcessingMode())
    2016                 :            :         {
    2017                 :            :                 /* In bootstrap mode, we have no indexes */
    2018                 :          0 :                 relation->rd_rel->relhasindex = false;
    2019                 :          0 :         }
    2020                 :            :         else
    2021                 :            :         {
    2022                 :            :                 /* Otherwise, all the rels formrdesc is used for have indexes */
    2023                 :        578 :                 relation->rd_rel->relhasindex = true;
    2024                 :            :         }
    2025                 :            : 
    2026                 :            :         /*
    2027                 :            :          * add new reldesc to relcache
    2028                 :            :          */
    2029                 :        578 :         RelationCacheInsert(relation);
    2030                 :            : 
    2031                 :            :         /* It's fully valid */
    2032                 :        578 :         relation->rd_isvalid = true;
    2033                 :        578 : }
    2034                 :            : 
    2035                 :            : 
    2036                 :            : static void
    2037                 :            : RelationInitAppendOnlyInfo(Relation relation)
    2038                 :            : {
    2039                 :            :         Relation        pg_appendonly_rel;
    2040                 :            :         HeapTuple       tuple;
    2041                 :            :         MemoryContext oldcontext;
    2042                 :            :         SysScanDesc scan;
    2043                 :            :         ScanKeyData skey;
    2044                 :            : 
    2045                 :            :         /*
    2046                 :            :          * Check the pg_appendonly relation to be certain the ao table
    2047                 :            :          * is there.
    2048                 :            :          */
    2049                 :     128844 :         pg_appendonly_rel = heap_open(AppendOnlyRelationId, AccessShareLock);
    2050                 :            : 
    2051                 :     128844 :         ScanKeyInit(&skey,
    2052                 :            :                                 Anum_pg_appendonly_relid,
    2053                 :            :                                 BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
    2054                 :     128844 :                                 ObjectIdGetDatum(RelationGetRelid(relation)));
    2055                 :            :         /* FIXME: isn't there a mode in relcache code to *not* use an index? Should
    2056                 :            :          * we do something here to obey it?
    2057                 :            :          */
    2058                 :     128844 :         scan = systable_beginscan(pg_appendonly_rel, AppendOnlyRelidIndexId, true,
    2059                 :            :                                                           SnapshotNow, 1, &skey);
    2060                 :            : 
    2061                 :     128844 :         tuple = systable_getnext(scan);
    2062         [ -  + ]:     128844 :         if (!tuple)
    2063                 :          0 :                 elog(ERROR, "could not find pg_appendonly tuple for relation \"%s\"",
    2064                 :            :                          RelationGetRelationName(relation));
    2065                 :            : 
    2066                 :            :         /*
    2067                 :            :          * Make a copy of the pg_appendonly entry for the table.
    2068                 :            :          */
    2069                 :     128844 :         oldcontext = MemoryContextSwitchTo(CacheMemoryContext);
    2070                 :     128844 :         relation->rd_aotuple = heap_copytuple(tuple);
    2071                 :     128844 :         relation->rd_appendonly = (Form_pg_appendonly) GETSTRUCT(relation->rd_aotuple);
    2072                 :     128844 :         MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcontext);
    2073                 :     128844 :         systable_endscan(scan);
    2074                 :     128844 :         heap_close(pg_appendonly_rel, AccessShareLock);
    2075                 :            : 
    2076                 :     128844 : }
    2077                 :            : 
    2078                 :            : 
    2079                 :            : /* ----------------------------------------------------------------
    2080                 :            :  *                               Relation Descriptor Lookup Interface
    2081                 :            :  * ----------------------------------------------------------------
    2082                 :            :  */
    2083                 :            : 
    2084                 :            : /*
    2085                 :            :  *              RelationIdGetRelation
    2086                 :            :  *
    2087                 :            :  *              Lookup a reldesc by OID; make one if not already in cache.
    2088                 :            :  *
    2089                 :            :  *              Returns NULL if no pg_class row could be found for the given relid
    2090                 :            :  *              (suggesting we are trying to access a just-deleted relation).
    2091                 :            :  *              Any other error is reported via elog.
    2092                 :            :  *
    2093                 :            :  *              NB: caller should already have at least AccessShareLock on the
    2094                 :            :  *              relation ID, else there are nasty race conditions.
    2095                 :            :  *
    2096                 :            :  *              NB: relation ref count is incremented, or set to 1 if new entry.
    2097                 :            :  *              Caller should eventually decrement count.  (Usually,
    2098                 :            :  *              that happens by calling RelationClose().)
    2099                 :            :  */
    2100                 :            : Relation
    2101                 :            : RelationIdGetRelation(Oid relationId)
    2102                 :            : {
    2103                 :            :         Relation        rd;
    2104                 :            : 
    2105                 :            :         /*
    2106                 :            :          * first try to find reldesc in the cache
    2107                 :            :          */
    2108         [ +  + ]:   34320019 :         RelationIdCacheLookup(relationId, rd);
    2109                 :            : 
    2110         [ +  + ]:   34320019 :         if (RelationIsValid(rd))
    2111                 :            :         {
    2112                 :   33774015 :                 RelationIncrementReferenceCount(rd);
    2113                 :            :                 /* revalidate cache entry if necessary */
    2114         [ +  + ]:   33774015 :                 if (!rd->rd_isvalid)
    2115                 :            :                 {
    2116                 :            :                         /*
    2117                 :            :                          * Indexes only have a limited number of possible schema changes,
    2118                 :            :                          * and we don't want to use the full-blown procedure because it's
    2119                 :            :                          * a headache for indexes that reload itself depends on.
    2120                 :            :                          */
    2121         [ +  - ]:     159455 :                         if (rd->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_INDEX)
    2122                 :     159455 :                                 RelationReloadIndexInfo(rd);
    2123                 :            :                         else
    2124                 :          0 :                                 RelationClearRelation(rd, true);
    2125                 :     159455 :                 }
    2126                 :   33774015 :                 return rd;
    2127                 :            :         }
    2128                 :            : 
    2129                 :            :         /*
    2130                 :            :          * no reldesc in the cache, so have RelationBuildDesc() build one and add
    2131                 :            :          * it.
    2132                 :            :          */
    2133                 :     546004 :         rd = RelationBuildDesc(relationId, true);
    2134         [ +  - ]:     546004 :         if (RelationIsValid(rd))
    2135                 :     546004 :                 RelationIncrementReferenceCount(rd);
    2136                 :            : 
    2137                 :     546004 :         return rd;
    2138                 :   34320019 : }
    2139                 :            : 
    2140                 :            : /* ----------------------------------------------------------------
    2141                 :            :  *                              cache invalidation support routines
    2142                 :            :  * ----------------------------------------------------------------
    2143                 :            :  */
    2144                 :            : 
    2145                 :            : /*
    2146                 :            :  * RelationIncrementReferenceCount
    2147                 :            :  *              Increments relation reference count.
    2148                 :            :  *
    2149                 :            :  * Note: bootstrap mode has its own weird ideas about relation refcount
    2150                 :            :  * behavior; we ought to fix it someday, but for now, just disable
    2151                 :            :  * reference count ownership tracking in bootstrap mode.
    2152                 :            :  */
    2153                 :            : void
    2154                 :            : RelationIncrementReferenceCount(Relation rel)
    2155                 :            : {
    2156                 :   71311179 :         ResourceOwnerEnlargeRelationRefs(CurrentResourceOwner);
    2157                 :   71311179 :         rel->rd_refcnt += 1;
    2158         [ +  - ]:   71311179 :         if (!IsBootstrapProcessingMode())
    2159                 :   71311179 :                 ResourceOwnerRememberRelationRef(CurrentResourceOwner, rel);
    2160                 :   71311179 : }
    2161                 :            : 
    2162                 :            : /*
    2163                 :            :  * RelationDecrementReferenceCount
    2164                 :            :  *              Decrements relation reference count.
    2165                 :            :  */
    2166                 :            : void
    2167                 :            : RelationDecrementReferenceCount(Relation rel)
    2168                 :            : {
    2169         [ -  + ]:   71311139 :         if (rel->rd_refcnt <= 0)
    2170                 :            :         {
    2171                 :          0 :                 elog(ERROR,
    2172                 :            :                          "Relation decrement reference count found relation %u/%u/%u with bad count (reference count %d)",
    2173                 :            :                          rel->rd_node.spcNode,
    2174                 :            :                          rel->rd_node.dbNode,
    2175                 :            :                          rel->rd_node.relNode,
    2176                 :            :                          rel->rd_refcnt);
    2177                 :          0 :         }
    2178                 :            :         
    2179                 :   71311139 :         rel->rd_refcnt -= 1;
    2180         [ +  - ]:   71311139 :         if (!IsBootstrapProcessingMode())
    2181                 :   71311139 :                 ResourceOwnerForgetRelationRef(CurrentResourceOwner, rel);
    2182                 :   71311139 : }
    2183                 :            : 
    2184                 :            : /*
    2185                 :            :  * RelationClose - close an open relation
    2186                 :            :  *
    2187                 :            :  *      Actually, we just decrement the refcount.
    2188                 :            :  *
    2189                 :            :  *      NOTE: if compiled with -DRELCACHE_FORCE_RELEASE then relcache entries
    2190                 :            :  *      will be freed as soon as their refcount goes to zero.  In combination
    2191                 :            :  *      with aset.c's CLOBBER_FREED_MEMORY option, this provides a good test
    2192                 :            :  *      to catch references to already-released relcache entries.  It slows
    2193                 :            :  *      things down quite a bit, however.
    2194                 :            :  */
    2195                 :            : void
    2196                 :            : RelationClose(Relation relation)
    2197                 :            : {
    2198                 :            :         /* Note: no locking manipulations needed */
    2199                 :   34458057 :         RelationDecrementReferenceCount(relation);
    2200                 :            : 
    2201                 :            : #ifdef RELCACHE_FORCE_RELEASE
    2202                 :            :         if (RelationHasReferenceCountZero(relation) &&
    2203                 :            :                 relation->rd_createSubid == InvalidSubTransactionId &&

    2209                 :            : /*
    2210                 :            :  * RelationReloadIndexInfo - reload minimal information for an open index
    2211                 :            :  *
    2212                 :            :  *      This function is used only for indexes.  A relcache inval on an index
    2213                 :            :  *      can mean that its pg_class or pg_index row changed.  There are only
    2214                 :            :  *      very limited changes that are allowed to an existing index's schema,
    2215                 :            :  *      so we can update the relcache entry without a complete rebuild; which
    2216                 :            :  *      is fortunate because we can't rebuild an index entry that is "nailed"
    2217                 :            :  *      and/or in active use.  We support full replacement of the pg_class row,
    2218                 :            :  *      as well as updates of a few simple fields of the pg_index row.
    2219                 :            :  *
    2220                 :            :  *      We can't necessarily reread the catalog rows right away; we might be
    2221                 :            :  *      in a failed transaction when we receive the SI notification.  If so,
    2222                 :            :  *      RelationClearRelation just marks the entry as invalid by setting
    2223                 :            :  *      rd_isvalid to false.  This routine is called to fix the entry when it
    2224                 :            :  *      is next needed.
    2225                 :            :  *
    2226                 :            :  *      We assume that at the time we are called, we have at least AccessShareLock
    2227                 :            :  *      on the target index.  (Note: in the calls from RelationClearRelation,
    2228                 :            :  *      this is legitimate because we know the rel has positive refcount.)
    2229                 :            :  *
    2230                 :            :  *      If the target index is an index on pg_class or pg_index, we'd better have
    2231                 :            :  *      previously gotten at least AccessShareLock on its underlying catalog,
    2232                 :            :  *      else we are at risk of deadlock against someone trying to exclusive-lock
    2233                 :            :  *      the heap and index in that order.  This is ensured in current usage by
    2234                 :            :  *      only applying this to indexes being opened or having positive refcount.
    2235                 :            :  */
    2236                 :            : static void
    2237                 :            : RelationReloadIndexInfo(Relation relation)
    2238                 :            : {
    2239                 :            :         bool            indexOK;
    2240                 :            :         HeapTuple       pg_class_tuple;
    2241                 :            :         Form_pg_class relp;
    2242                 :            : 
    2243                 :            :         /* Should be called only for invalidated indexes */
    2244 [ +  - ][ +  - ]:     224700 :         Assert(relation->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_INDEX &&
                 [ -  + ]
    2245                 :            :                    !relation->rd_isvalid);
    2246                 :            :         /* Should be closed at smgr level */
    2247 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:     224700 :         Assert(relation->rd_smgr == NULL);
    2248                 :            : 
    2249                 :            :         /* Make sure targblock is reset in case rel was truncated */
    2250                 :     224700 :         relation->rd_targblock = InvalidBlockNumber;
    2251                 :            :         /* Must free any AM cached data, too */
    2252         [ +  + ]:     224700 :         if (relation->rd_amcache)
    2253                 :     137595 :                 pfree(relation->rd_amcache);
    2254                 :     224700 :         relation->rd_amcache = NULL;
    2255                 :            : 
    2256                 :            :         /*
    2257                 :            :          * If it's a shared index, we might be called before backend startup has
    2258                 :            :          * finished selecting a database, in which case we have no way to read
    2259                 :            :          * pg_class yet.  However, a shared index can never have any significant
    2260                 :            :          * schema updates, so it's okay to ignore the invalidation signal.  Just
    2261                 :            :          * mark it valid and return without doing anything more.
    2262                 :            :          */
    2263 [ +  + ][ -  + ]:     224700 :         if (relation->rd_rel->relisshared && !criticalRelcachesBuilt)
    2264                 :            :         {
    2265                 :          0 :                 relation->rd_isvalid = true;
    2266                 :          0 :                 return;
    2267                 :            :         }
    2268                 :            : 
    2269                 :            :         /*
    2270                 :            :          * Read the pg_class row
    2271                 :            :          *
    2272                 :            :          * Don't try to use an indexscan of pg_class_oid_index to reload the info
    2273                 :            :          * for pg_class_oid_index ...
    2274                 :            :          */
    2275                 :     224700 :         indexOK = (RelationGetRelid(relation) != ClassOidIndexId);
    2276                 :     224700 :         pg_class_tuple = ScanPgRelation(RelationGetRelid(relation), indexOK, NULL);
    2277         [ -  + ]:     224700 :         if (!HeapTupleIsValid(pg_class_tuple))
    2278                 :          0 :                 elog(ERROR, "could not find pg_class tuple for index %u",
    2279                 :            :                          RelationGetRelid(relation));
    2280                 :     224700 :         relp = (Form_pg_class) GETSTRUCT(pg_class_tuple);
    2281                 :     224700 :         memcpy(relation->rd_rel, relp, CLASS_TUPLE_SIZE);
    2282                 :            :         /* Reload reloptions in case they changed */
    2283         [ +  + ]:     224700 :         if (relation->rd_options)
    2284                 :        240 :                 pfree(relation->rd_options);
    2285                 :     224700 :         RelationParseRelOptions(relation, pg_class_tuple);
    2286                 :            :         /* done with pg_class tuple */
    2287                 :     224700 :         heap_freetuple(pg_class_tuple);
    2288                 :            :         /* We must recalculate physical address in case it changed */
    2289                 :     224700 :         RelationInitPhysicalAddr(relation);
    2290                 :            : 
    2291                 :            :         /* Forget gp_relation_node information -- it may have changed. */
    2292 [ +  - ][ +  - ]:     674100 :         MemSet(&relation->rd_segfile0_relationnodeinfo, 0, sizeof(RelationNodeInfo));
         [ +  - ][ +  - ]
                 [ +  + ]
    2293                 :            : 
    2294                 :            :         /*
    2295                 :            :          * For a non-system index, there are fields of the pg_index row that are
    2296                 :            :          * allowed to change, so re-read that row and update the relcache entry.
    2297                 :            :          * Most of the info derived from pg_index (such as support function lookup
    2298                 :            :          * info) cannot change, and indeed the whole point of this routine is to
    2299                 :            :          * update the relcache entry without clobbering that data; so wholesale
    2300                 :            :          * replacement is not appropriate.
    2301                 :            :          */
    2302         [ +  + ]:     224700 :         if (!IsSystemRelation(relation))
    2303                 :            :         {
    2304                 :            :                 HeapTuple       tuple;
    2305                 :            :                 Form_pg_index index;
    2306                 :            : 
    2307                 :      22726 :                 tuple = SearchSysCache(INDEXRELID,
    2308                 :      22726 :                                                            ObjectIdGetDatum(RelationGetRelid(relation)),
    2309                 :            :                                                            0, 0, 0);
    2310         [ -  + ]:      22726 :                 if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tuple))
    2311                 :          0 :                         elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for index %u",
    2312                 :            :                                  RelationGetRelid(relation));
    2313                 :      22726 :                 index = (Form_pg_index) GETSTRUCT(tuple);
    2314                 :            : 
    2315                 :            :                 /*
    2316                 :            :                  * Basically, let's just copy all the bool fields.  There are one or
    2317                 :            :                  * two of these that can't actually change in the current code, but
    2318                 :            :                  * it's not worth it to track exactly which ones they are.  None of
    2319                 :            :                  * the array fields are allowed to change, though.
    2320                 :            :                  */
    2321                 :      22726 :                 relation->rd_index->indisunique = index->indisunique;
    2322                 :      22726 :                 relation->rd_index->indisprimary = index->indisprimary;
    2323                 :      22726 :                 relation->rd_index->indisclustered = index->indisclustered;
    2324                 :      22726 :                 relation->rd_index->indisvalid = index->indisvalid;
    2325                 :      22726 :                 relation->rd_index->indcheckxmin = index->indcheckxmin;
    2326                 :      22726 :                 relation->rd_index->indisready = index->indisready;
    2327                 :            : 
    2328                 :            :                 /* Copy xmin too, as that is needed to make sense of indcheckxmin */
    2329                 :      22726 :                 HeapTupleHeaderSetXmin(relation->rd_indextuple->t_data,
    2330                 :            :                                                            HeapTupleHeaderGetXmin(tuple->t_data));
    2331                 :            : 
    2332                 :      22726 :                 ReleaseSysCache(tuple);
    2333                 :      22726 :         }
    2334                 :            : 
    2335                 :            :         /* Okay, now it's valid again */
    2336                 :     224700 :         relation->rd_isvalid = true;
    2337                 :     224700 : }
    2338                 :            : 
    2339                 :            : /*
    2340                 :            :  * RelationDestroyRelation
    2341                 :            :  *
    2342                 :            :  *      Physically delete a relation cache entry and all subsidiary data.
    2343                 :            :  *      Caller must already have unhooked the entry from the hash table.
    2344                 :            :  */
    2345                 :            : static void
    2346                 :            : RelationDestroyRelation(Relation relation)
    2347                 :            : {
    2348 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:    1929931 :         Assert(RelationHasReferenceCountZero(relation));
    2349                 :            : 
    2350                 :            :         /*
    2351                 :            :          * Make sure smgr and lower levels close the relation's files, if they
    2352                 :            :          * weren't closed already.  (This was probably done by caller, but let's
    2353                 :            :          * just be real sure.)
    2354                 :            :          */
    2355 [ -  + ][ #  # ]:    1929931 :         RelationCloseSmgr(relation);
                 [ #  # ]
    2356                 :            : 
    2357                 :            :         /*
    2358                 :            :          * Free all the subsidiary data structures of the relcache entry,
    2359                 :            :          * then the entry itself.
    2360                 :            :          */
    2361         [ +  - ]:    1929931 :         if (relation->rd_rel)
    2362                 :    1929931 :                 pfree(relation->rd_rel);
    2363                 :            :         /* can't use DecrTupleDescRefCount here */
    2364 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:    1929931 :         Assert(relation->rd_att->tdrefcount > 0);
    2365         [ +  + ]:    1929931 :         if (--relation->rd_att->tdrefcount == 0)
    2366                 :    1929445 :                 FreeTupleDesc(relation->rd_att);
    2367                 :    1929931 :         list_free(relation->rd_indexlist);
    2368                 :    1929931 :         bms_free(relation->rd_indexattr);
    2369                 :    1929931 :         FreeTriggerDesc(relation->trigdesc);
    2370         [ +  + ]:    1929931 :         if (relation->rd_options)
    2371                 :     132915 :                 pfree(relation->rd_options);
    2372         [ +  + ]:    1929931 :         if (relation->rd_indextuple)
    2373                 :     757695 :                 pfree(relation->rd_indextuple);
    2374         [ +  + ]:    1929931 :         if (relation->rd_aotuple)
    2375                 :     126301 :                 pfree(relation->rd_aotuple);
    2376         [ +  + ]:    1929931 :         if (relation->rd_am)
    2377                 :     757695 :                 pfree(relation->rd_am);
    2378         [ +  + ]:    1929931 :         if (relation->rd_indexcxt)

    2380         [ +  + ]:    1929931 :         if (relation->rd_rulescxt)

    2382         [ +  + ]:    1929931 :         if (relation->rd_cdbpolicy)
    2383                 :     401795 :                 pfree(relation->rd_cdbpolicy);
    2384                 :            : 
    2385                 :    1929931 :         pfree(relation);
    2386                 :    1929931 : }
    2387                 :            : 
    2388                 :            : /*
    2389                 :            :  * RelationClearRelation
    2390                 :            :  *
    2391                 :            :  *       Physically blow away a relation cache entry, or reset it and rebuild
    2392                 :            :  *       it from scratch (that is, from catalog entries).  The latter path is
    2393                 :            :  *       used when we are notified of a change to an open relation (one with
    2394                 :            :  *       refcount > 0).
    2395                 :            :  *
    2396                 :            :  *       NB: when rebuilding, we'd better hold some lock on the relation,
    2397                 :            :  *       else the catalog data we need to read could be changing under us.
    2398                 :            :  *       Also, a rel to be rebuilt had better have refcnt > 0.  This is because
    2399                 :            :  *       an sinval reset could happen while we're accessing the catalogs, and
    2400                 :            :  *       the rel would get blown away underneath us by RelationCacheInvalidate
    2401                 :            :  *       if it has zero refcnt.
    2402                 :            :  *
    2403                 :            :  *       The "rebuild" parameter is redundant in current usage because it has
    2404                 :            :  *       to match the relation's refcnt status, but we keep it as a crosscheck
    2405                 :            :  *       that we're doing what the caller expects.
    2406                 :            :  */
    2407                 :            : static void
    2408                 :            : RelationClearRelation(Relation relation, bool rebuild)
    2409                 :            : {
    2410                 :            :         /*
    2411                 :            :          * As per notes above, a rel to be rebuilt MUST have refcnt > 0; while
    2412                 :            :          * of course it would be a bad idea to blow away one with nonzero refcnt.
    2413                 :            :          */
    2414 [ +  - ][ +  + ]:    3889059 :         Assert(rebuild ?
         [ -  + ][ -  + ]
    2415                 :            :                    !RelationHasReferenceCountZero(relation) :
    2416                 :            :                    RelationHasReferenceCountZero(relation));
    2417                 :            : 
    2418                 :            :         /*
    2419                 :            :          * Make sure smgr and lower levels close the relation's files, if they
    2420                 :            :          * weren't closed already.  If the relation is not getting deleted, the
    2421                 :            :          * next smgr access should reopen the files automatically.      This ensures
    2422                 :            :          * that the low-level file access state is updated after, say, a vacuum
    2423                 :            :          * truncation.
    2424                 :            :          */
    2425 [ +  + ][ +  - ]:    3889059 :         RelationCloseSmgr(relation);
                 [ -  + ]
    2426                 :            : 
    2427                 :            :         /*
    2428                 :            :          * Never, never ever blow away a nailed-in system relation, because we'd
    2429                 :            :          * be unable to recover.  However, we must reset rd_targblock, in case we
    2430                 :            :          * got called because of a relation cache flush that was triggered by
    2431                 :            :          * VACUUM.  Likewise reset the fsm and vm size info.
    2432                 :            :          *
    2433                 :            :          * If it's a nailed index, then we need to re-read the pg_class row to see
    2434                 :            :          * if its relfilenode changed.  We can't necessarily do that here, because
    2435                 :            :          * we might be in a failed transaction.  We assume it's okay to do it if
    2436                 :            :          * there are open references to the relcache entry (cf notes for
    2437                 :            :          * AtEOXact_RelationCache).  Otherwise just mark the entry as possibly
    2438                 :            :          * invalid, and it'll be fixed when next opened.
    2439                 :            :          */
    2440         [ +  + ]:    3889059 :         if (relation->rd_isnailed)
    2441                 :            :         {
    2442                 :    1893963 :                 relation->rd_targblock = InvalidBlockNumber;
    2443         [ +  + ]:    1893963 :                 if (relation->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_INDEX)
    2444                 :            :                 {
    2445                 :    1262674 :                         relation->rd_isvalid = false;                /* needs to be revalidated */
    2446         [ +  + ]:    1262674 :                         if (relation->rd_refcnt > 1)
    2447                 :         80 :                                 RelationReloadIndexInfo(relation);
    2448                 :    1262674 :                 }
    2449                 :    1893963 :                 return;
    2450                 :            :         }
    2451                 :            : 
    2452                 :            :         /*
    2453                 :            :          * Even non-system indexes should not be blown away if they are open and
    2454                 :            :          * have valid index support information.  This avoids problems with active
    2455                 :            :          * use of the index support information.  As with nailed indexes, we
    2456                 :            :          * re-read the pg_class row to handle possible physical relocation of the
    2457                 :            :          * index, and we check for pg_index updates too.
    2458                 :            :          */
    2459 [ +  + ][ +  + ]:    1995096 :         if (relation->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_INDEX &&
    2460         [ +  + ]:     869027 :                 relation->rd_refcnt > 0 &&
    2461                 :     111329 :                 relation->rd_indexcxt != NULL)
    2462                 :            :         {
    2463                 :      65165 :                 relation->rd_isvalid = false;        /* needs to be revalidated */
    2464                 :      65165 :                 RelationReloadIndexInfo(relation);
    2465                 :      65165 :                 return;
    2466                 :            :         }
    2467                 :            : 
    2468                 :            :         /* Mark it invalid until we've finished rebuild */
    2469                 :    1929931 :         relation->rd_isvalid = false;
    2470                 :            : 
    2471                 :            :         /*
    2472                 :            :          * If we're really done with the relcache entry, blow it away. But if
    2473                 :            :          * someone is still using it, reconstruct the whole deal without moving
    2474                 :            :          * the physical RelationData record (so that the someone's pointer is
    2475                 :            :          * still valid).
    2476                 :            :          */
    2477         [ +  + ]:    1929931 :         if (!rebuild)
    2478                 :            :         {
    2479                 :            :                 /* Remove it from the hash table */
    2480         [ -  + ]:    1595971 :                 RelationCacheDelete(relation);
    2481                 :            : 
    2482                 :            :                 /* And release storage */
    2483                 :    1595971 :                 RelationDestroyRelation(relation);
    2484                 :    1595971 :         }
    2485                 :            :         else
    2486                 :            :         {
    2487                 :            :                 /*
    2488                 :            :                  * Our strategy for rebuilding an open relcache entry is to build
    2489                 :            :                  * a new entry from scratch, swap its contents with the old entry,
    2490                 :            :                  * and finally delete the new entry (along with any infrastructure
    2491                 :            :                  * swapped over from the old entry).  This is to avoid trouble in case
    2492                 :            :                  * an error causes us to lose control partway through.  The old entry
    2493                 :            :                  * will still be marked !rd_isvalid, so we'll try to rebuild it again
    2494                 :            :                  * on next access.  Meanwhile it's not any less valid than it was
    2495                 :            :                  * before, so any code that might expect to continue accessing it
    2496                 :            :                  * isn't hurt by the rebuild failure.  (Consider for example a
    2497                 :            :                  * subtransaction that ALTERs a table and then gets cancelled partway
    2498                 :            :                  * through the cache entry rebuild.  The outer transaction should
    2499                 :            :                  * still see the not-modified cache entry as valid.)  The worst
    2500                 :            :                  * consequence of an error is leaking the necessarily-unreferenced
    2501                 :            :                  * new entry, and this shouldn't happen often enough for that to be
    2502                 :            :                  * a big problem.
    2503                 :            :                  *
    2504                 :            :                  * When rebuilding an open relcache entry, we must preserve ref count
    2505                 :            :                  * and rd_createSubid/rd_newRelfilenodeSubid state.  Also attempt to
    2506                 :            :                  * preserve the pg_class entry (rd_rel), tupledesc, and rewrite-rule
    2507                 :            :                  * substructures in place, because various places assume that these
    2508                 :            :                  * structures won't move while they are working with an open relcache
    2509                 :            :                  * entry.  (Note: the refcount mechanism for tupledescs might someday
    2510                 :            :                  * allow us to remove this hack for the tupledesc.)
    2511                 :            :                  *
    2512                 :            :                  * Note that this process does not touch CurrentResourceOwner; which
    2513                 :            :                  * is good because whatever ref counts the entry may have do not
    2514                 :            :                  * necessarily belong to that resource owner.
    2515                 :            :                  */
    2516                 :            :                 Relation        newrel;
    2517                 :     333960 :                 Oid                     save_relid = RelationGetRelid(relation);
    2518                 :            :                 bool            keep_tupdesc;
    2519                 :            :                 bool            keep_rules;
    2520                 :            :                 bool            keep_pt_info;
    2521                 :            : 
    2522                 :            :                 /* Build temporary entry, but don't link it into hashtable */
    2523                 :     333960 :                 newrel = RelationBuildDesc(save_relid, false);
    2524         [ -  + ]:     333960 :                 if (newrel == NULL)
    2525                 :            :                 {
    2526                 :            :                         /* Should only get here if relation was deleted */
    2527         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         RelationCacheDelete(relation);
    2528                 :          0 :                         RelationDestroyRelation(relation);
    2529                 :          0 :                         elog(ERROR, "relation %u deleted while still in use", save_relid);
    2530                 :          0 :                 }
    2531                 :            :  
    2532                 :     333960 :                 keep_tupdesc = equalTupleDescs(relation->rd_att, newrel->rd_att, true);
    2533                 :     333960 :                 keep_rules = equalRuleLocks(relation->rd_rules, newrel->rd_rules);
    2534                 :     333960 :                 keep_pt_info = (relation->rd_rel->relfilenode ==
    2535                 :     333960 :                                                 newrel->rd_rel->relfilenode);
    2536                 :            : 
    2537                 :            :                 /*
    2538                 :            :                  * Perform swapping of the relcache entry contents.  Within this
    2539                 :            :                  * process the old entry is momentarily invalid, so there *must*
    2540                 :            :                  * be no possibility of CHECK_FOR_INTERRUPTS within this sequence.
    2541                 :            :                  * Do it in all-in-line code for safety.
    2542                 :            :                  *
    2543                 :            :                  * Since the vast majority of fields should be swapped, our method
    2544                 :            :                  * is to swap the whole structures and then re-swap those few fields
    2545                 :            :                  * we didn't want swapped.
    2546                 :            :                  */
    2547                 :            : #define SWAPFIELD(fldtype, fldname) \
    2548                 :            :                 do { \
    2549                 :            :                         fldtype _tmp = newrel->fldname; \
    2550                 :            :                         newrel->fldname = relation->fldname; \
    2551                 :            :                         relation->fldname = _tmp; \
    2552                 :            :                 } while (0)
    2553                 :            : 
    2554                 :            :                 /* swap all Relation struct fields */
    2555                 :            :                 {
    2556                 :            :                         RelationData tmpstruct;
    2557                 :            : 
    2558                 :     333960 :                         memcpy(&tmpstruct, newrel, sizeof(RelationData));
    2559                 :     333960 :                         memcpy(newrel, relation, sizeof(RelationData));
    2560                 :     333960 :                         memcpy(relation, &tmpstruct, sizeof(RelationData));
    2561                 :            :                 }
    2562                 :            : 
    2563                 :            :                 /* rd_smgr must not be swapped, due to back-links from smgr level */
    2564                 :     333960 :                 SWAPFIELD(SMgrRelation, rd_smgr);
    2565                 :            :                 /* rd_refcnt must be preserved */
    2566                 :     333960 :                 SWAPFIELD(int, rd_refcnt);
    2567                 :            :                 /* isnailed shouldn't change */
    2568 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:     333960 :                 Assert(newrel->rd_isnailed == relation->rd_isnailed);
    2569                 :            :                 /* creation sub-XIDs must be preserved */
    2570                 :     333960 :                 SWAPFIELD(SubTransactionId, rd_createSubid);
    2571                 :     333960 :                 SWAPFIELD(SubTransactionId, rd_newRelfilenodeSubid);
    2572                 :            :                 /* un-swap rd_rel pointers, swap contents instead */
    2573                 :     333960 :                 SWAPFIELD(Form_pg_class, rd_rel);
    2574                 :            :                 /* ... but actually, we don't have to update newrel->rd_rel */
    2575                 :     333960 :                 memcpy(relation->rd_rel, newrel->rd_rel, CLASS_TUPLE_SIZE);
    2576                 :            :                 /* preserve old tupledesc and rules if no logical change */
    2577         [ +  + ]:     333960 :                 if (keep_tupdesc)
    2578                 :     233166 :                         SWAPFIELD(TupleDesc, rd_att);
    2579         [ +  + ]:     333960 :                 if (keep_rules)
    2580                 :            :                 {
    2581                 :     333417 :                         SWAPFIELD(RuleLock *, rd_rules);
    2582                 :     333417 :                         SWAPFIELD(MemoryContext, rd_rulescxt);
    2583                 :     333417 :                 }
    2584                 :            :                 /* pgstat_info must be preserved */
    2585                 :     333960 :                 SWAPFIELD(struct PgStat_TableStatus *, pgstat_info);
    2586                 :            : 
    2587                 :            :                 /* preserve persistent table information for the relation  */
    2588         [ +  + ]:     333960 :                 if (keep_pt_info)
    2589                 :     319339 :                         SWAPFIELD(struct RelationNodeInfo, rd_segfile0_relationnodeinfo);
    2590                 :            : 
    2591                 :            : #undef SWAPFIELD
    2592                 :            : 
    2593                 :            :                 /* And now we can throw away the temporary entry */
    2594                 :     333960 :                 RelationDestroyRelation(newrel);
    2595                 :            :         }
    2596                 :    3889059 : }
    2597                 :            : 
    2598                 :            : /*
    2599                 :            :  * RelationFlushRelation
    2600                 :            :  *
    2601                 :            :  *       Rebuild the relation if it is open (refcount > 0), else blow it away.
    2602                 :            :  */
    2603                 :            : static void
    2604                 :            : RelationFlushRelation(Relation relation)
    2605                 :            : {
    2606 [ +  + ][ +  + ]:     637391 :         if (relation->rd_createSubid != InvalidSubTransactionId ||
    2607                 :     292400 :                 relation->rd_newRelfilenodeSubid != InvalidSubTransactionId)
    2608                 :            :         {
    2609                 :            :                 /*
    2610                 :            :                  * New relcache entries are always rebuilt, not flushed; else we'd
    2611                 :            :                  * forget the "new" status of the relation, which is a useful
    2612                 :            :                  * optimization to have.  Ditto for the new-relfilenode status.
    2613                 :            :                  *
    2614                 :            :                  * The rel could have zero refcnt here, so temporarily increment
    2615                 :            :                  * the refcnt to ensure it's safe to rebuild it.  We can assume that
    2616                 :            :                  * the current transaction has some lock on the rel already.
    2617                 :            :                  */
    2618                 :     356206 :                 RelationIncrementReferenceCount(relation);
    2619                 :     356206 :                 RelationClearRelation(relation, true);
    2620                 :     356206 :                 RelationDecrementReferenceCount(relation);
    2621                 :     356206 :         }
    2622                 :            :         else
    2623                 :            :         {
    2624                 :            :                 /*
    2625                 :            :                  * Pre-existing rels can be dropped from the relcache if not open.
    2626                 :            :                  */
    2627                 :     281185 :                 bool    rebuild = !RelationHasReferenceCountZero(relation);
    2628                 :            : 
    2629                 :     281185 :                 RelationClearRelation(relation, rebuild);
    2630                 :            :         }
    2631                 :     637391 : }
    2632                 :            : 
    2633                 :            : /*
    2634                 :            :  * RelationForgetRelation - unconditionally remove a relcache entry
    2635                 :            :  *
    2636                 :            :  *                 External interface for destroying a relcache entry when we
    2637                 :            :  *                 drop the relation.
    2638                 :            :  */
    2639                 :            : void
    2640                 :            : RelationForgetRelation(Oid rid)
    2641                 :            : {
    2642                 :            :         Relation        relation;
    2643                 :            : 
    2644         [ +  + ]:      95687 :         RelationIdCacheLookup(rid, relation);
    2645                 :            : 
    2646         [ +  + ]:      95687 :         if (!PointerIsValid(relation))
    2647                 :       5073 :                 return;                                 /* not in cache, nothing to do */
    2648                 :            : 
    2649         [ -  + ]:      90614 :         if (!RelationHasReferenceCountZero(relation))
    2650                 :          0 :                 elog(ERROR, "relation %u is still open", rid);
    2651                 :            : 
    2652                 :            :         /* Unconditionally destroy the relcache entry */
    2653                 :      90614 :         RelationClearRelation(relation, false);
    2654                 :      95687 : }
    2655                 :            : 
    2656                 :            : /*
    2657                 :            :  *              RelationCacheInvalidateEntry
    2658                 :            :  *
    2659                 :            :  *              This routine is invoked for SI cache flush messages.
    2660                 :            :  *
    2661                 :            :  * Any relcache entry matching the relid must be flushed.  (Note: caller has
    2662                 :            :  * already determined that the relid belongs to our database or is a shared
    2663                 :            :  * relation.)
    2664                 :            :  *
    2665                 :            :  * We used to skip local relations, on the grounds that they could
    2666                 :            :  * not be targets of cross-backend SI update messages; but it seems
    2667                 :            :  * safer to process them, so that our *own* SI update messages will
    2668                 :            :  * have the same effects during CommandCounterIncrement for both
    2669                 :            :  * local and nonlocal relations.
    2670                 :            :  */
    2671                 :            : void
    2672                 :            : RelationCacheInvalidateEntry(Oid relationId)
    2673                 :            : {
    2674                 :            :         Relation        relation;
    2675                 :            : 
    2676         [ +  + ]:    4236594 :         RelationIdCacheLookup(relationId, relation);
    2677                 :            : 
    2678         [ +  + ]:    4236594 :         if (PointerIsValid(relation))
    2679                 :            :         {
    2680                 :     637391 :                 relcacheInvalsReceived++;
    2681                 :     637391 :                 RelationFlushRelation(relation);
    2682                 :     637391 :         }
    2683                 :    4236594 : }
    2684                 :            : 
    2685                 :            : /*
    2686                 :            :  * RelationCacheInvalidate
    2687                 :            :  *       Blow away cached relation descriptors that have zero reference counts,
    2688                 :            :  *       and rebuild those with positive reference counts.      Also reset the smgr
    2689                 :            :  *       relation cache.
    2690                 :            :  *
    2691                 :            :  *       This is currently used only to recover from SI message buffer overflow,
    2692                 :            :  *       so we do not touch new-in-transaction relations; they cannot be targets
    2693                 :            :  *       of cross-backend SI updates (and our own updates now go through a
    2694                 :            :  *       separate linked list that isn't limited by the SI message buffer size).
    2695                 :            :  *       Likewise, we need not discard new-relfilenode-in-transaction hints,
    2696                 :            :  *       since any invalidation of those would be a local event.
    2697                 :            :  *
    2698                 :            :  *       We do this in two phases: the first pass deletes deletable items, and
    2699                 :            :  *       the second one rebuilds the rebuildable items.  This is essential for
    2700                 :            :  *       safety, because hash_seq_search only copes with concurrent deletion of
    2701                 :            :  *       the element it is currently visiting.  If a second SI overflow were to
    2702                 :            :  *       occur while we are walking the table, resulting in recursive entry to
    2703                 :            :  *       this routine, we could crash because the inner invocation blows away
    2704                 :            :  *       the entry next to be visited by the outer scan.  But this way is OK,
    2705                 :            :  *       because (a) during the first pass we won't process any more SI messages,
    2706                 :            :  *       so hash_seq_search will complete safely; (b) during the second pass we
    2707                 :            :  *       only hold onto pointers to nondeletable entries.
    2708                 :            :  *
    2709                 :            :  *       The two-phase approach also makes it easy to ensure that we process
    2710                 :            :  *       nailed-in-cache indexes before other nondeletable items, and that we
    2711                 :            :  *       process pg_class_oid_index first of all.  In scenarios where a nailed
    2712                 :            :  *       index has been given a new relfilenode, we have to detect that update
    2713                 :            :  *       before the nailed index is used in reloading any other relcache entry.
    2714                 :            :  */
    2715                 :            : void
    2716                 :            : RelationCacheInvalidate(void)
    2717                 :            : {
    2718                 :            :         HASH_SEQ_STATUS status;
    2719                 :            :         RelIdCacheEnt *idhentry;
    2720                 :            :         Relation        relation;
    2721                 :       1691 :         List       *rebuildFirstList = NIL;
    2722                 :       1691 :         List       *rebuildList = NIL;
    2723                 :            :         ListCell   *l;
    2724                 :            : 
    2725                 :            :         /* Phase 1 */
    2726                 :       1691 :         hash_seq_init(&status, RelationIdCache);
    2727                 :            : 
    2728         [ +  + ]:     102839 :         while ((idhentry = (RelIdCacheEnt *) hash_seq_search(&status)) != NULL)
    2729                 :            :         {
    2730                 :     101148 :                 relation = idhentry->reldesc;
    2731                 :            : 
    2732                 :            :                 /* Must close all smgr references to avoid leaving dangling ptrs */
    2733 [ +  + ][ +  - ]:     101148 :                 RelationCloseSmgr(relation);
                 [ -  + ]
    2734                 :            : 
    2735                 :            :                 /* Ignore new relations, since they are never SI targets */
    2736         [ +  + ]:     101148 :                 if (relation->rd_createSubid != InvalidSubTransactionId)
    2737                 :       2463 :                         continue;
    2738                 :            : 
    2739                 :      98685 :                 relcacheInvalsReceived++;
    2740                 :            : 
    2741         [ +  + ]:      98685 :                 if (RelationHasReferenceCountZero(relation))
    2742                 :            :                 {
    2743                 :            :                         /* Delete this entry immediately */
    2744 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:      63068 :                         Assert(!relation->rd_isnailed);
    2745                 :      63068 :                         RelationClearRelation(relation, false);
    2746                 :      63068 :                 }
    2747                 :            :                 else
    2748                 :            :                 {
    2749                 :            :                         /*
    2750                 :            :                          * Add this entry to list of stuff to rebuild in second pass.
    2751                 :            :                          * pg_class_oid_index goes on the front of rebuildFirstList, other
    2752                 :            :                          * nailed indexes on the back, and everything else into
    2753                 :            :                          * rebuildList (in no particular order).
    2754                 :            :                          */
    2755 [ +  + ][ +  + ]:      35617 :                         if (relation->rd_isnailed &&
    2756                 :      35511 :                                 relation->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_INDEX)
    2757                 :            :                         {
    2758         [ +  + ]:      23674 :                                 if (RelationGetRelid(relation) == ClassOidIndexId)
    2759                 :       1691 :                                         rebuildFirstList = lcons(relation, rebuildFirstList);
    2760                 :            :                                 else
    2761                 :      21983 :                                         rebuildFirstList = lappend(rebuildFirstList, relation);
    2762                 :      23674 :                         }
    2763                 :            :                         else
    2764                 :      11943 :                                 rebuildList = lcons(relation, rebuildList);
    2765                 :            :                 }
    2766                 :            :         }
    2767                 :            : 
    2768                 :            :         /*
    2769                 :            :          * Now zap any remaining smgr cache entries.  This must happen before we
    2770                 :            :          * start to rebuild entries, since that may involve catalog fetches which
    2771                 :            :          * will re-open catalog files.
    2772                 :            :          */
    2773                 :       1691 :         smgrcloseall();
    2774                 :            : 
    2775                 :            :         /* Phase 2: rebuild the items found to need rebuild in phase 1 */
    2776         [ +  + ]:      25365 :         foreach(l, rebuildFirstList)
    2777                 :            :         {
    2778                 :      23674 :                 relation = (Relation) lfirst(l);
    2779                 :      23674 :                 RelationClearRelation(relation, true);
    2780                 :      23674 :         }
    2781                 :       1691 :         list_free(rebuildFirstList);
    2782         [ +  + ]:      13634 :         foreach(l, rebuildList)
    2783                 :            :         {
    2784                 :      11943 :                 relation = (Relation) lfirst(l);
    2785                 :      11943 :                 RelationClearRelation(relation, true);
    2786                 :      11943 :         }
    2787                 :       1691 :         list_free(rebuildList);
    2788                 :       1691 : }
    2789                 :            : 
    2790                 :            : /*
    2791                 :            :  * AtEOXact_RelationCache
    2792                 :            :  *
    2793                 :            :  *      Clean up the relcache at main-transaction commit or abort.
    2794                 :            :  *
    2795                 :            :  * Note: this must be called *before* processing invalidation messages.
    2796                 :            :  * In the case of abort, we don't want to try to rebuild any invalidated
    2797                 :            :  * cache entries (since we can't safely do database accesses).  Therefore
    2798                 :            :  * we must reset refcnts before handling pending invalidations.
    2799                 :            :  *
    2800                 :            :  * As of PostgreSQL 8.1, relcache refcnts should get released by the
    2801                 :            :  * ResourceOwner mechanism.  This routine just does a debugging
    2802                 :            :  * cross-check that no pins remain.  However, we also need to do special
    2803                 :            :  * cleanup when the current transaction created any relations or made use
    2804                 :            :  * of forced index lists.
    2805                 :            :  */
    2806                 :            : void
    2807                 :            : AtEOXact_RelationCache(bool isCommit)
    2808                 :            : {
    2809                 :            :         HASH_SEQ_STATUS status;
    2810                 :            :         RelIdCacheEnt *idhentry;
    2811                 :            : 
    2812                 :            :         /*
    2813                 :            :          * To speed up transaction exit, we want to avoid scanning the relcache
    2814                 :            :          * unless there is actually something for this routine to do.  Other than
    2815                 :            :          * the debug-only Assert checks, most transactions don't create any work
    2816                 :            :          * for us to do here, so we keep a static flag that gets set if there is
    2817                 :            :          * anything to do.      (Currently, this means either a relation is created in
    2818                 :            :          * the current xact, or one is given a new relfilenode, or an index list
    2819                 :            :          * is forced.)  For simplicity, the flag remains set till end of top-level
    2820                 :            :          * transaction, even though we could clear it at subtransaction end in
    2821                 :            :          * some cases.
    2822                 :            :          *
    2823                 :            :          * MPP-3333: READERS need to *always* scan, otherwise they will not be able
    2824                 :            :          * to maintain a coherent view of the storage layer.
    2825                 :            :          */
    2826         [ -  + ]:     344434 :         if (!need_eoxact_work
    2827         [ +  + ]:     344434 :                 && DistributedTransactionContext != DTX_CONTEXT_QE_READER
    2828                 :            : #ifdef USE_ASSERT_CHECKING
    2829         [ +  + ]:     322790 :                 && !assert_enabled
    2830                 :            : #endif
    2831                 :            :                 )
    2832                 :          0 :                 return;
    2833                 :            : 
    2834                 :     344434 :         hash_seq_init(&status, RelationIdCache);
    2835                 :            : 
    2836         [ +  + ]:   32239007 :         while ((idhentry = (RelIdCacheEnt *) hash_seq_search(&status)) != NULL)
    2837                 :            :         {
    2838                 :   31894573 :                 Relation        relation = idhentry->reldesc;
    2839                 :            : 
    2840                 :            :                 /*
    2841                 :            :                  * The relcache entry's ref count should be back to its normal
    2842                 :            :                  * not-in-a-transaction state: 0 unless it's nailed in cache.
    2843                 :            :                  *
    2844                 :            :                  * In bootstrap mode, this is NOT true, so don't check it --- the
    2845                 :            :                  * bootstrap code expects relations to stay open across start/commit
    2846                 :            :                  * transaction calls.  (That seems bogus, but it's not worth fixing.)
    2847                 :            :                  */
    2848                 :            : #ifdef USE_ASSERT_CHECKING
