    2849         [ +  - ]:   31894573 :                 if (!IsBootstrapProcessingMode())
    2850                 :            :                 {
    2851                 :            :                         int                     expected_refcnt;
    2852                 :            : 
    2853                 :   31894573 :                         expected_refcnt = relation->rd_isnailed ? 1 : 0;
    2854 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:   31894573 :                         Assert(relation->rd_refcnt == expected_refcnt);
    2855                 :   31894573 :                 }
    2856                 :            : #endif
    2857                 :            : 
    2858                 :            :                 /*
    2859                 :            :                  * QE-readers aren't properly enrolled in transactions, they
    2860                 :            :                  * just get the snapshot which corresponds -- so here, where
    2861                 :            :                  * we are maintaining their relcache, we want to just clean
    2862                 :            :                  * up (almost as if we had aborted). (MPP-3338)
    2863                 :            :                  */
    2864 [ +  + ][ +  + ]:   31894573 :                 if (DistributedTransactionContext == DTX_CONTEXT_QE_ENTRY_DB_SINGLETON ||
    2865                 :   31742611 :                         DistributedTransactionContext == DTX_CONTEXT_QE_READER)
    2866                 :            :                 {
    2867                 :    3061723 :                         RelationClearRelation(relation, relation->rd_isnailed ? true : false);
    2868                 :    3061723 :                         continue;
    2869                 :            :                 }
    2870                 :            : 
    2871                 :            :                 /*
    2872                 :            :                  * Is it a relation created in the current transaction?
    2873                 :            :                  *
    2874                 :            :                  * During commit, reset the flag to zero, since we are now out of the
    2875                 :            :                  * creating transaction.  During abort, simply delete the relcache
    2876                 :            :                  * entry --- it isn't interesting any longer.  (NOTE: if we have
    2877                 :            :                  * forgotten the new-ness of a new relation due to a forced cache
    2878                 :            :                  * flush, the entry will get deleted anyway by shared-cache-inval
    2879                 :            :                  * processing of the aborted pg_class insertion.)
    2880                 :            :                  */
    2881         [ +  + ]:   28832850 :                 if (relation->rd_createSubid != InvalidSubTransactionId)
    2882                 :            :                 {
    2883         [ +  + ]:     128586 :                         if (isCommit)
    2884                 :     128048 :                                 relation->rd_createSubid = InvalidSubTransactionId;
    2885                 :            :                         else
    2886                 :            :                         {
    2887                 :            :                                 /*
    2888                 :            :                                  * In abort, delete the error log file before forgetting
    2889                 :            :                                  * this relation.
    2890                 :            :                                  */
    2891                 :        538 :                                 ErrorLogDelete(MyDatabaseId, RelationGetRelid(relation));
    2892                 :        538 :                                 RelationClearRelation(relation, false);
    2893                 :        538 :                                 continue;
    2894                 :            :                         }
    2895                 :     128048 :                 }
    2896                 :            : 
    2897                 :            :                 /*
    2898                 :            :                  * Likewise, reset the hint about the relfilenode being new.
    2899                 :            :                  */
    2900                 :   28832312 :                 relation->rd_newRelfilenodeSubid = InvalidSubTransactionId;
    2901                 :            : 
    2902                 :            :                 /*
    2903                 :            :                  * Flush any temporary index list.
    2904                 :            :                  */
    2905         [ +  + ]:   28832312 :                 if (relation->rd_indexvalid == 2)
    2906                 :            :                 {
    2907                 :          8 :                         list_free(relation->rd_indexlist);
    2908                 :          8 :                         relation->rd_indexlist = NIL;
    2909                 :          8 :                         relation->rd_oidindex = InvalidOid;
    2910                 :          8 :                         relation->rd_indexvalid = 0;
    2911                 :          8 :                 }
    2912                 :            :         }
    2913                 :            : 
    2914                 :            :         /* Once done with the transaction, we can reset need_eoxact_work */
    2915                 :     344434 :         need_eoxact_work = false;
    2916                 :     344434 : }
    2917                 :            : 
    2918                 :            : /*
    2919                 :            :  * AtEOSubXact_RelationCache
    2920                 :            :  *
    2921                 :            :  *      Clean up the relcache at sub-transaction commit or abort.
    2922                 :            :  *
    2923                 :            :  * Note: this must be called *before* processing invalidation messages.
    2924                 :            :  */
    2925                 :            : void
    2926                 :            : AtEOSubXact_RelationCache(bool isCommit, SubTransactionId mySubid,
    2927                 :            :                                                   SubTransactionId parentSubid)
    2928                 :            : {
    2929                 :            :         HASH_SEQ_STATUS status;
    2930                 :            :         RelIdCacheEnt *idhentry;
    2931                 :            : 
    2932                 :            :         /*
    2933                 :            :          * Skip the relcache scan if nothing to do --- see notes for
    2934                 :            :          * AtEOXact_RelationCache.
    2935                 :            :          */
    2936 [ +  + ][ +  + ]:       8006 :         if (!need_eoxact_work &&
    2937                 :       6280 :                 DistributedTransactionContext != DTX_CONTEXT_QE_READER)
    2938                 :       6259 :                 return;
    2939                 :            : 
    2940                 :       1747 :         hash_seq_init(&status, RelationIdCache);
    2941                 :            : 
    2942         [ +  + ]:     173746 :         while ((idhentry = (RelIdCacheEnt *) hash_seq_search(&status)) != NULL)
    2943                 :            :         {
    2944                 :     171999 :                 Relation        relation = idhentry->reldesc;
    2945                 :            : 
    2946                 :            :                 /*
    2947                 :            :                  * As opposed to AtEOXact_RelationCache, subtransactions
    2948                 :            :                  * in readers are only caused by internal commands, and
    2949                 :            :                  * there shouldn't be interaction with global transactions,
    2950                 :            :                  * (reader gangs commit their transaction independently)
    2951                 :            :                  * we must not clear the relcache here.
    2952                 :            :                  */
    2953                 :            : 
    2954                 :            :                 /*
    2955                 :            :                  * Is it a relation created in the current subtransaction?
    2956                 :            :                  *
    2957                 :            :                  * During subcommit, mark it as belonging to the parent, instead.
    2958                 :            :                  * During subabort, simply delete the relcache entry.
    2959                 :            :                  */
    2960         [ +  + ]:     171999 :                 if (relation->rd_createSubid == mySubid)
    2961                 :            :                 {
    2962         [ +  + ]:        304 :                         if (isCommit)
    2963                 :        196 :                                 relation->rd_createSubid = parentSubid;
    2964         [ +  - ]:        108 :                         else if (RelationHasReferenceCountZero(relation))
    2965                 :            :                         {
    2966                 :            :                                 /*
    2967                 :            :                                  * In abort, delete the error log file before forgetting
    2968                 :            :                                  * this relation.
    2969                 :            :                                  */
    2970                 :        108 :                                 ErrorLogDelete(MyDatabaseId, RelationGetRelid(relation));
    2971                 :            : 
    2972                 :        108 :                                 RelationClearRelation(relation, false);
    2973                 :        108 :                                 continue;
    2974                 :            :                         }
    2975                 :            :                         else
    2976                 :            :                         {
    2977                 :            :                                 /*
    2978                 :            :                                  * Hmm, somewhere there's a (leaked?) reference to the
    2979                 :            :                                  * relation.  We daren't remove the entry for fear of
    2980                 :            :                                  * dereferencing a dangling pointer later.  Bleat, and mark it
    2981                 :            :                                  * as not belonging to the current transaction.  Hopefully
    2982                 :            :                                  * it'll get cleaned up eventually.  This must be just a
    2983                 :            :                                  * WARNING to avoid error-during-error-recovery loops.
    2984                 :            :                                  */
    2985                 :          0 :                                 relation->rd_createSubid = InvalidSubTransactionId;
    2986                 :          0 :                                 elog(WARNING, "cannot remove relcache entry for \"%s\" because it has nonzero refcount",
    2987                 :            :                                          RelationGetRelationName(relation));
    2988                 :            :                         }
    2989                 :        196 :                 }
    2990                 :            : 
    2991                 :            :                 /*
    2992                 :            :                  * Likewise, update or drop any new-relfilenode-in-subtransaction
    2993                 :            :                  * hint.
    2994                 :            :                  */
    2995         [ +  + ]:     171891 :                 if (relation->rd_newRelfilenodeSubid == mySubid)
    2996                 :            :                 {
    2997         [ +  + ]:        636 :                         if (isCommit)
    2998                 :        408 :                                 relation->rd_newRelfilenodeSubid = parentSubid;
    2999                 :            :                         else
    3000                 :        228 :                                 relation->rd_newRelfilenodeSubid = InvalidSubTransactionId;
    3001                 :        636 :                 }
    3002                 :            : 
    3003                 :            :                 /*
    3004                 :            :                  * Flush any temporary index list.
    3005                 :            :                  */
    3006         [ -  + ]:     171891 :                 if (relation->rd_indexvalid == 2)
    3007                 :            :                 {
    3008                 :          0 :                         list_free(relation->rd_indexlist);
    3009                 :          0 :                         relation->rd_indexlist = NIL;
    3010                 :          0 :                         relation->rd_oidindex = InvalidOid;
    3011                 :          0 :                         relation->rd_indexvalid = 0;
    3012                 :          0 :                 }
    3013                 :            :         }
    3014                 :       8006 : }
    3015                 :            : 
    3016                 :            : /*
    3017                 :            :  * RelationCacheMarkNewRelfilenode
    3018                 :            :  *
    3019                 :            :  *      Mark the rel as having been given a new relfilenode in the current
    3020                 :            :  *      (sub) transaction.      This is a hint that can be used to optimize
    3021                 :            :  *      later operations on the rel in the same transaction.
    3022                 :            :  */
    3023                 :            : void
    3024                 :            : RelationCacheMarkNewRelfilenode(Relation rel)
    3025                 :            : {
    3026                 :            :         /* Mark it... */
    3027                 :      18161 :         rel->rd_newRelfilenodeSubid = GetCurrentSubTransactionId();
    3028                 :            :         /* ... and now we have eoxact cleanup work to do */
    3029                 :      18161 :         need_eoxact_work = true;
    3030                 :      18161 : }
    3031                 :            : 
    3032                 :            : 
    3033                 :            : /*
    3034                 :            :  *              RelationBuildLocalRelation
    3035                 :            :  *                      Build a relcache entry for an about-to-be-created relation,
    3036                 :            :  *                      and enter it into the relcache.
    3037                 :            :  */
    3038                 :            : Relation
    3039                 :            : RelationBuildLocalRelation(const char *relname,
    3040                 :            :                                                    Oid relnamespace,
    3041                 :            :                                                    TupleDesc tupDesc,
    3042                 :            :                                                    Oid relid,
    3043                 :            :                                                    Oid reltablespace,
    3044                 :            :                                        char relkind,            /*CDB*/
    3045                 :            :                                                    bool shared_relation)
    3046                 :            : {
    3047                 :            :         Relation        rel;
    3048                 :            :         MemoryContext oldcxt;
    3049                 :     136531 :         int                     natts = tupDesc->natts;
    3050                 :            :         int                     i;
    3051                 :            :         bool            has_not_null;
    3052                 :            :         bool            nailit;
    3053                 :            : 
    3054 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:     136531 :         AssertArg(natts >= 0);
    3055                 :            : 
    3056                 :            :         /*
    3057                 :            :          * check for creation of a rel that must be nailed in cache.
    3058                 :            :          *
    3059                 :            :          * XXX this list had better match the relations specially handled in
    3060                 :            :          * RelationCacheInitializePhase2/3.
    3061                 :            :          */
    3062   [ -  -  -  -  :     136531 :         switch (relid)
             -  -  -  + ]
    3063                 :            :         {
    3064                 :            :                 case DatabaseRelationId:
    3065                 :            :                 case AuthIdRelationId:
    3066                 :            :                 case AuthMemRelationId:
    3067                 :            :                 case RelationRelationId:
    3068                 :            :                 case AttributeRelationId:
    3069                 :            :                 case ProcedureRelationId:
    3070                 :            :                 case TypeRelationId:
    3071                 :          0 :                         nailit = true;
    3072                 :          0 :                         break;
    3073                 :            :                 default:
    3074                 :     136531 :                         nailit = false;
    3075                 :     136531 :                         break;
    3076                 :            :         }
    3077                 :            : 
    3078                 :            :         /*
    3079                 :            :          * check that hardwired list of shared rels matches what's in the
    3080                 :            :          * bootstrap .bki file.  If you get a failure here during initdb, you
    3081                 :            :          * probably need to fix IsSharedRelation() to match whatever you've done
    3082                 :            :          * to the set of shared relations.
    3083                 :            :          */
    3084         [ -  + ]:     136531 :         if (shared_relation != IsSharedRelation(relid))
    3085                 :          0 :                 elog(ERROR, "shared_relation flag for \"%s\" does not match IsSharedRelation(%u)",
    3086                 :            :                          relname, relid);
    3087                 :            : 
    3088                 :            :         /*
    3089                 :            :          * switch to the cache context to create the relcache entry.
    3090                 :            :          */
    3091         [ -  + ]:     136531 :         if (!CacheMemoryContext)
    3092                 :          0 :                 CreateCacheMemoryContext();
    3093                 :            : 
    3094                 :     136531 :         oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(CacheMemoryContext);
    3095                 :            : 
    3096                 :            :         /*
    3097                 :            :          * allocate a new relation descriptor and fill in basic state fields.
    3098                 :            :          */
    3099                 :     136531 :         rel = (Relation) palloc0(sizeof(RelationData));
    3100                 :            : 
    3101                 :     136531 :         rel->rd_targblock = InvalidBlockNumber;
    3102                 :            : 
    3103                 :            :         /* make sure relation is marked as having no open file yet */
    3104                 :     136531 :         rel->rd_smgr = NULL;
    3105                 :            : 
    3106                 :            :         /* mark it nailed if appropriate */
    3107                 :     136531 :         rel->rd_isnailed = nailit;
    3108                 :            : 
    3109                 :     136531 :         rel->rd_refcnt = nailit ? 1 : 0;
    3110                 :            : 
    3111                 :            :         /* it's being created in this transaction */
    3112                 :     136531 :         rel->rd_createSubid = GetCurrentSubTransactionId();
    3113                 :     136531 :         rel->rd_newRelfilenodeSubid = InvalidSubTransactionId;
    3114                 :            : 
    3115                 :            :         /* must flag that we have rels created in this transaction */
    3116                 :     136531 :         need_eoxact_work = true;
    3117                 :            : 
    3118                 :            :         /* is it a temporary relation? */
    3119                 :     136531 :         rel->rd_istemp = isTempOrToastNamespace(relnamespace);
    3120                 :            : 
    3121                 :            :         /* is it a system catalog? */
    3122                 :     136531 :         rel->rd_issyscat = (strncmp(relname, "pg_", 3) == 0);
    3123                 :            : 
    3124                 :            :         /*
    3125                 :            :          * CDB: On QEs, temp relations must use shared buffer cache so data
    3126                 :            :          * will be visible to all segmates.  On QD, sequence objects must
    3127                 :            :          * use shared buffer cache so data will be visible to sequence server.
    3128                 :            :          */
    3129 [ +  + ][ +  + ]:     136531 :         if (rel->rd_istemp &&
    3130         [ +  + ]:       1779 :                 relkind != RELKIND_SEQUENCE &&
    3131                 :       1739 :                 Gp_role != GP_ROLE_EXECUTE)
    3132                 :        437 :                 rel->rd_isLocalBuf = true;
    3133                 :            :         else
    3134                 :     136094 :                 rel->rd_isLocalBuf = false;
    3135                 :            : 
    3136                 :            :         /*
    3137                 :            :          * create a new tuple descriptor from the one passed in.  We do this
    3138                 :            :          * partly to copy it into the cache context, and partly because the new
    3139                 :            :          * relation can't have any defaults or constraints yet; they have to be
    3140                 :            :          * added in later steps, because they require additions to multiple system
    3141                 :            :          * catalogs.  We can copy attnotnull constraints here, however.
    3142                 :            :          */
    3143                 :     136531 :         rel->rd_att = CreateTupleDescCopy(tupDesc);
    3144                 :     136531 :         rel->rd_att->tdrefcount = 1;      /* mark as refcounted */
    3145                 :     136531 :         has_not_null = false;
    3146         [ +  + ]:     701960 :         for (i = 0; i < natts; i++)
    3147                 :            :         {
    3148                 :     565429 :                 rel->rd_att->attrs[i]->attnotnull = tupDesc->attrs[i]->attnotnull;
    3149                 :     565429 :                 has_not_null |= tupDesc->attrs[i]->attnotnull;
    3150                 :     565429 :         }
    3151                 :            : 
    3152         [ +  + ]:     136531 :         if (has_not_null)
    3153                 :            :         {
    3154                 :       5112 :                 TupleConstr *constr = (TupleConstr *) palloc0(sizeof(TupleConstr));
    3155                 :            : 
    3156                 :       5112 :                 constr->has_not_null = true;
    3157                 :       5112 :                 rel->rd_att->constr = constr;
    3158                 :       5112 :         }
    3159                 :            : 
    3160                 :            :         /*
    3161                 :            :          * initialize relation tuple form (caller may add/override data later)
    3162                 :            :          */
    3163                 :     136531 :         rel->rd_rel = (Form_pg_class) palloc0(CLASS_TUPLE_SIZE);
    3164                 :            : 
    3165                 :     136531 :         namestrcpy(&rel->rd_rel->relname, relname);
    3166                 :     136531 :         rel->rd_rel->relnamespace = relnamespace;
    3167                 :            : 
    3168                 :     136531 :         rel->rd_rel->relkind = RELKIND_UNCATALOGED;
    3169                 :     136531 :         rel->rd_rel->relstorage = RELSTORAGE_HEAP;
    3170                 :     136531 :         rel->rd_rel->relhasoids = rel->rd_att->tdhasoid;
    3171                 :     136531 :         rel->rd_rel->relnatts = natts;
    3172                 :     136531 :         rel->rd_rel->reltype = InvalidOid;
    3173                 :            :         /* needed when bootstrapping: */
    3174                 :     136531 :         rel->rd_rel->relowner = BOOTSTRAP_SUPERUSERID;
    3175                 :            : 
    3176                 :            :         /*
    3177                 :            :          * Create zeroed-out gp_relation_node data.  It will be filled in when the
    3178                 :            :          * disk file is created.
    3179                 :            :          */
    3180                 :     136531 :         rel->rd_segfile0_relationnodeinfo.isPresent = false;
    3181                 :     136531 :         rel->rd_segfile0_relationnodeinfo.tidAllowedToBeZero = false;
    3182                 :            : 
    3183                 :            :         /*
    3184                 :            :          * Insert relation physical and logical identifiers (OIDs) into the right
    3185                 :            :          * places.
    3186                 :            :          *
    3187                 :            :          * In PostgreSQL, the physical ID (relfilenode) is initially the same
    3188                 :            :          * as the logical ID (OID). In GPDB, the table's logical OID is allocated
    3189                 :            :          * in the master, and might already be in use as a relfilenode of an
    3190                 :            :          * existing relation in a segment.
    3191                 :            :          *
    3192                 :            :          * In binary upgrade mode, however, use the OID also as the relfilenode.
    3193                 :            :          * pg_upgrade gets confused if they don't match.
    3194                 :            :          */
    3195                 :     136531 :         rel->rd_rel->relisshared = shared_relation;
    3196                 :            : 
    3197                 :     136531 :         RelationGetRelid(rel) = relid;
    3198                 :            : 
    3199         [ +  + ]:     701960 :         for (i = 0; i < natts; i++)
    3200                 :     565429 :                 rel->rd_att->attrs[i]->attrelid = relid;
    3201                 :            : 
    3202         [ -  + ]:     136531 :         if (relid < FirstNormalObjectId /* bootstrap only */
    3203 [ +  - ][ +  + ]:     136531 :                 || (Gp_role != GP_ROLE_EXECUTE && relkind == RELKIND_SEQUENCE)
    3204         [ +  + ]:     136385 :                 || IsBinaryUpgrade)
    3205                 :        146 :                 rel->rd_rel->relfilenode = relid;
    3206                 :            :         else
    3207                 :            :         {
    3208                 :     136385 :                 rel->rd_rel->relfilenode = GetNewRelFileNode(reltablespace, shared_relation);
    3209         [ +  + ]:     136385 :                 if (Gp_role == GP_ROLE_EXECUTE)
    3210                 :     102391 :                         AdvanceObjectId(relid);
    3211                 :            :         }
    3212                 :            : 
    3213                 :     136531 :         rel->rd_rel->reltablespace = reltablespace;
    3214                 :            : 
    3215                 :     136531 :         RelationInitLockInfo(rel);      /* see lmgr.c */
    3216                 :            : 
    3217                 :     136531 :         RelationInitPhysicalAddr(rel);
    3218                 :            : 
    3219                 :            :         /*
    3220                 :            :          * Okay to insert into the relcache hash tables.
    3221                 :            :          */
    3222                 :     136531 :         RelationCacheInsert(rel);
    3223                 :            : 
    3224                 :            :         /*
    3225                 :            :          * done building relcache entry.
    3226                 :            :          */
    3227                 :     136531 :         MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcxt);
    3228                 :            : 
    3229                 :            :         /* It's fully valid */
    3230                 :     136531 :         rel->rd_isvalid = true;
    3231                 :            : 
    3232                 :            :         /*
    3233                 :            :          * Caller expects us to pin the returned entry.
    3234                 :            :          */
    3235                 :     136531 :         RelationIncrementReferenceCount(rel);
    3236                 :            : 
    3237                 :     136531 :         return rel;
    3238                 :            : }
    3239                 :            : 
    3240                 :            : /*
    3241                 :            :  *              RelationCacheInitialize
    3242                 :            :  *
    3243                 :            :  *              This initializes the relation descriptor cache.  At the time
    3244                 :            :  *              that this is invoked, we can't do database access yet (mainly
    3245                 :            :  *              because the transaction subsystem is not up); all we are doing
    3246                 :            :  *              is making an empty cache hashtable.  This must be done before
    3247                 :            :  *              starting the initialization transaction, because otherwise
    3248                 :            :  *              AtEOXact_RelationCache would crash if that transaction aborts
    3249                 :            :  *              before we can get the relcache set up.
    3250                 :            :  */
    3251                 :            : 
    3252                 :            : #define INITRELCACHESIZE                400
    3253                 :            : 
    3254                 :            : void
    3255                 :            : RelationCacheInitialize(void)
    3256                 :            : {
    3257                 :            :         MemoryContext oldcxt;
    3258                 :            :         HASHCTL         ctl;
    3259                 :            : 
    3260                 :            :         /*
    3261                 :            :          * make sure cache memory context exists
    3262                 :            :          */
    3263         [ +  - ]:      17712 :         if (!CacheMemoryContext)
    3264                 :      17712 :                 CreateCacheMemoryContext();
    3265                 :            : 
    3266                 :            :     /*
    3267                 :            :          * switch to cache memory context
    3268                 :            :          */
    3269                 :      17712 :         oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(CacheMemoryContext);
    3270                 :            : 
    3271                 :            :         /*
    3272                 :            :          * create hashtable that indexes the relcache
    3273                 :            :          */
    3274 [ +  - ][ +  - ]:     247968 :         MemSet(&ctl, 0, sizeof(ctl));
         [ +  - ][ +  - ]
                 [ +  + ]
    3275                 :      17712 :         ctl.keysize = sizeof(Oid);
    3276                 :      17712 :         ctl.entrysize = sizeof(RelIdCacheEnt);
    3277                 :      17712 :         ctl.hash = oid_hash;
    3278                 :      17712 :         RelationIdCache = hash_create("Relcache by OID", INITRELCACHESIZE,
    3279                 :            :                                                                   &ctl, HASH_ELEM | HASH_FUNCTION);
    3280                 :            : 
    3281                 :      17712 :         MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcxt);
    3282                 :      17712 : }
    3283                 :            : 
    3284                 :            : /*
    3285                 :            :  *              RelationCacheInitializePhase2
    3286                 :            :  *
    3287                 :            :  *              This is called to prepare for access to shared catalogs during startup.
    3288                 :            :  *              We must at least set up nailed reldescs for pg_database, pg_authid,
    3289                 :            :  *              and pg_auth_members.  Ideally we'd like to have reldescs for their
    3290                 :            :  *              indexes, too.  We attempt to load this information from the shared
    3291                 :            :  *              relcache init file.  If that's missing or broken, just make phony
    3292                 :            :  *              entries for the catalogs themselves.  RelationCacheInitializePhase3
    3293                 :            :  *              will clean up as needed.
    3294                 :            :  */
    3295                 :            : void
    3296                 :            : RelationCacheInitializePhase2(void)
    3297                 :            : {
    3298                 :            :         MemoryContext oldcxt;
    3299                 :            : 
    3300                 :            :         /*
    3301                 :            :          * In bootstrap mode, the shared catalogs aren't there yet anyway, so do
    3302                 :            :          * nothing.
    3303                 :            :          */
    3304         [ -  + ]:      17712 :         if (IsBootstrapProcessingMode())
    3305                 :          0 :                 return;
    3306                 :            : 
    3307                 :            :         /*
    3308                 :            :          * switch to cache memory context
    3309                 :            :          */
    3310                 :      17712 :         oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(CacheMemoryContext);
    3311                 :            : 
    3312                 :            :         /*
    3313                 :            :          * Try to load the shared relcache cache file.  If unsuccessful, bootstrap
    3314                 :            :          * the cache with pre-made descriptors for the critical shared catalogs.
    3315                 :            :          */
    3316         [ +  + ]:      17712 :         if (!load_relcache_init_file(true))
    3317                 :            :         {
    3318                 :         22 :                 formrdesc("pg_database", PG_DATABASE_RELTYPE_OID, true,
    3319                 :            :                                   true, Natts_pg_database, Desc_pg_database);
    3320                 :         22 :                 formrdesc("pg_authid", PG_AUTHID_RELTYPE_OID, true,
    3321                 :            :                                   true, Natts_pg_authid, Desc_pg_authid);
    3322                 :         22 :                 formrdesc("pg_auth_members", PG_AUTH_MEMBERS_RELTYPE_OID, true,
    3323                 :            :                                   false, Natts_pg_auth_members, Desc_pg_auth_members);
    3324                 :            : 
    3325                 :            : #define NUM_CRITICAL_SHARED_RELS        3       /* fix if you change list above */
    3326                 :         22 :         }
    3327                 :            : 
    3328                 :      17712 :         MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcxt);
    3329                 :      17712 : }
    3330                 :            : 
    3331                 :            : /*
    3332                 :            :  *              RelationCacheInitializePhase3
    3333                 :            :  *
    3334                 :            :  *              This is called as soon as the catcache and transaction system
    3335                 :            :  *              are functional and we have determined MyDatabaseId.  At this point
    3336                 :            :  *              we can actually read data from the database's system catalogs.
    3337                 :            :  *              We first try to read pre-computed relcache entries from the local
    3338                 :            :  *              relcache init file.  If that's missing or broken, make phony entries
    3339                 :            :  *              for the minimum set of nailed-in-cache relations.  Then (unless
    3340                 :            :  *              bootstrapping) make sure we have entries for the critical system
    3341                 :            :  *              indexes.  Once we've done all this, we have enough infrastructure to
    3342                 :            :  *              open any system catalog or use any catcache.  The last step is to
    3343                 :            :  *              rewrite the cache files if needed.
    3344                 :            :  */
    3345                 :            : void
    3346                 :            : RelationCacheInitializePhase3(void)
    3347                 :            : {
    3348                 :            :         HASH_SEQ_STATUS status;
    3349                 :            :         RelIdCacheEnt *idhentry;
    3350                 :            :         MemoryContext oldcxt;
    3351                 :      17683 :         bool            needNewCacheFile = !criticalSharedRelcachesBuilt;
    3352                 :            : 
    3353                 :            :         /*
    3354                 :            :          * Relation cache initialization or any sort of heap access is
    3355                 :            :          * dangerous before recovery is finished.
    3356                 :            :          */
    3357 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:      17683 :         if (!IsBootstrapProcessingMode() && RecoveryInProgress())
    3358                 :          0 :                 elog(ERROR, "relation cache initialization during recovery or non-bootstrap processes.");
    3359                 :            : 
    3360                 :            :         /*
    3361                 :            :          * switch to cache memory context
    3362                 :            :          */
    3363                 :      17683 :         oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(CacheMemoryContext);
    3364                 :            : 
    3365                 :            :         /*
    3366                 :            :          * Try to load the local relcache cache file.  If unsuccessful, bootstrap
    3367                 :            :          * the cache with pre-made descriptors for the critical "nailed-in" system
    3368                 :            :          * catalogs.
    3369                 :            :          */
    3370 [ +  - ][ +  + ]:      17683 :         if (IsBootstrapProcessingMode() ||
    3371                 :      17683 :                 !load_relcache_init_file(false))
    3372                 :            :         {
    3373                 :        128 :                 needNewCacheFile = true;
    3374                 :            : 
    3375                 :        128 :                 formrdesc("pg_class", PG_CLASS_RELTYPE_OID, false,
    3376                 :            :                                   true, Natts_pg_class, Desc_pg_class);
    3377                 :        128 :                 formrdesc("pg_attribute", PG_ATTRIBUTE_RELTYPE_OID, false,
    3378                 :            :                                   false, Natts_pg_attribute, Desc_pg_attribute);
    3379                 :        128 :                 formrdesc("pg_proc", PG_PROC_RELTYPE_OID, false,
    3380                 :            :                                   true, Natts_pg_proc, Desc_pg_proc);
    3381                 :        128 :                 formrdesc("pg_type", PG_TYPE_RELTYPE_OID, false,
    3382                 :            :                                   true, Natts_pg_type, Desc_pg_type);
    3383                 :            : 
    3384                 :            : #define NUM_CRITICAL_LOCAL_RELS 4               /* fix if you change list above */
    3385                 :        128 :         }
    3386                 :            : 
    3387                 :      17683 :         MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcxt);
    3388                 :            : 
    3389                 :            :         /* In bootstrap mode, the faked-up formrdesc info is all we'll have */
    3390         [ -  + ]:      17683 :         if (IsBootstrapProcessingMode())
    3391                 :          0 :                 return;
    3392                 :            : 
    3393                 :            :         /*
    3394                 :            :          * If we didn't get the critical system indexes loaded into relcache, do
    3395                 :            :          * so now.      These are critical because the catcache and/or opclass cache
    3396                 :            :          * depend on them for fetches done during relcache load.  Thus, we have an
    3397                 :            :          * infinite-recursion problem.  We can break the recursion by doing
    3398                 :            :          * heapscans instead of indexscans at certain key spots. To avoid hobbling
    3399                 :            :          * performance, we only want to do that until we have the critical indexes
    3400                 :            :          * loaded into relcache.  Thus, the flag criticalRelcachesBuilt is used to
    3401                 :            :          * decide whether to do heapscan or indexscan at the key spots, and we set
    3402                 :            :          * it true after we've loaded the critical indexes.
    3403                 :            :          *
    3404                 :            :          * The critical indexes are marked as "nailed in cache", partly to make it
    3405                 :            :          * easy for load_relcache_init_file to count them, but mainly because we
    3406                 :            :          * cannot flush and rebuild them once we've set criticalRelcachesBuilt to
    3407                 :            :          * true.  (NOTE: perhaps it would be possible to reload them by
    3408                 :            :          * temporarily setting criticalRelcachesBuilt to false again.  For now,
    3409                 :            :          * though, we just nail 'em in.)
    3410                 :            :          *
    3411                 :            :          * RewriteRelRulenameIndexId and TriggerRelidNameIndexId are not critical
    3412                 :            :          * in the same way as the others, because the critical catalogs don't
    3413                 :            :          * (currently) have any rules or triggers, and so these indexes can be
    3414                 :            :          * rebuilt without inducing recursion.  However they are used during
    3415                 :            :          * relcache load when a rel does have rules or triggers, so we choose to
    3416                 :            :          * nail them for performance reasons.
    3417                 :            :          */
    3418         [ +  + ]:      17683 :         if (!criticalRelcachesBuilt)
    3419                 :            :         {
    3420                 :        128 :                 load_critical_index(ClassOidIndexId,
    3421                 :            :                                                         RelationRelationId);
    3422                 :        128 :                 load_critical_index(AttributeRelidNumIndexId,
    3423                 :            :                                                         AttributeRelationId);
    3424                 :        128 :                 load_critical_index(IndexRelidIndexId,
    3425                 :            :                                                         IndexRelationId);
    3426                 :        128 :                 load_critical_index(AccessMethodStrategyIndexId,
    3427                 :            :                                                         AccessMethodOperatorRelationId);
    3428                 :        128 :                 load_critical_index(OpclassOidIndexId,
    3429                 :            :                                                         OperatorClassRelationId);
    3430                 :        128 :                 load_critical_index(AccessMethodProcedureIndexId,
    3431                 :            :                                                         AccessMethodProcedureRelationId);
    3432                 :        128 :                 load_critical_index(OperatorOidIndexId,
    3433                 :            :                                                         OperatorRelationId);
    3434                 :        128 :                 load_critical_index(RewriteRelRulenameIndexId,
    3435                 :            :                                                         RewriteRelationId);
    3436                 :        128 :                 load_critical_index(TriggerRelidNameIndexId,
    3437                 :            :                                                         TriggerRelationId);
    3438                 :            : 
    3439                 :            : #define NUM_CRITICAL_LOCAL_INDEXES      9       /* fix if you change list above */
    3440                 :            : 
    3441                 :        128 :                 criticalRelcachesBuilt = true;
    3442                 :        128 :         }
    3443                 :            : 
    3444                 :            :         /*
    3445                 :            :          * Process critical shared indexes too.
    3446                 :            :          *
    3447                 :            :          * DatabaseNameIndexId isn't critical for relcache loading, but rather for
    3448                 :            :          * initial lookup of MyDatabaseId, without which we'll never find any
    3449                 :            :          * non-shared catalogs at all.  Autovacuum calls InitPostgres with a
    3450                 :            :          * database OID, so it instead depends on DatabaseOidIndexId.  We also
    3451                 :            :          * need to nail up some indexes on pg_authid and pg_auth_members for use
    3452                 :            :          * during client authentication.
    3453                 :            :          */
    3454         [ +  + ]:      17683 :         if (!criticalSharedRelcachesBuilt)
    3455                 :            :         {
    3456                 :         12 :                 load_critical_index(DatabaseNameIndexId,
    3457                 :            :                                                         DatabaseRelationId);
    3458                 :         12 :                 load_critical_index(DatabaseOidIndexId,
    3459                 :            :                                                         DatabaseRelationId);
    3460                 :         12 :                 load_critical_index(AuthIdRolnameIndexId,
    3461                 :            :                                                         AuthIdRelationId);
    3462                 :         12 :                 load_critical_index(AuthIdOidIndexId,
    3463                 :            :                                                         AuthIdRelationId);
    3464                 :         12 :                 load_critical_index(AuthMemMemRoleIndexId,
    3465                 :            :                                                         AuthMemRelationId);
    3466                 :            : 
    3467                 :            : #define NUM_CRITICAL_SHARED_INDEXES 5   /* fix if you change list above */
    3468                 :            : 
    3469                 :         12 :                 criticalSharedRelcachesBuilt = true;
    3470                 :         12 :         }
    3471                 :            : 
    3472                 :            :         /*
    3473                 :            :          * Now, scan all the relcache entries and update anything that might be
    3474                 :            :          * wrong in the results from formrdesc or the relcache cache file. If we
    3475                 :            :          * faked up relcache entries using formrdesc, then read the real pg_class
    3476                 :            :          * rows and replace the fake entries with them. Also, if any of the
    3477                 :            :          * relcache entries have rules or triggers, load that info the hard way
    3478                 :            :          * since it isn't recorded in the cache file.
    3479                 :            :          *
    3480                 :            :          * Whenever we access the catalogs to read data, there is a possibility
    3481                 :            :          * of a shared-inval cache flush causing relcache entries to be removed.
    3482                 :            :          * Since hash_seq_search only guarantees to still work after the *current*
    3483                 :            :          * entry is removed, it's unsafe to continue the hashtable scan afterward.
    3484                 :            :          * We handle this by restarting the scan from scratch after each access.
    3485                 :            :          * This is theoretically O(N^2), but the number of entries that actually
    3486                 :            :          * need to be fixed is small enough that it doesn't matter.
    3487                 :            :          */
    3488                 :      17683 :         hash_seq_init(&status, RelationIdCache);
    3489                 :            : 
    3490         [ +  + ]:    3915570 :         while ((idhentry = (RelIdCacheEnt *) hash_seq_search(&status)) != NULL)
    3491                 :            :         {
    3492                 :    3897887 :                 Relation        relation = idhentry->reldesc;
    3493                 :    3897887 :                 bool            restart = false;
    3494                 :            : 
    3495                 :            :                 /*
    3496                 :            :                  * Make sure *this* entry doesn't get flushed while we work with it.
    3497                 :            :                  */
    3498                 :    3897887 :                 RelationIncrementReferenceCount(relation);
    3499                 :            : 
    3500                 :            :                 /*
    3501                 :            :                  * If it's a faked-up entry, read the real pg_class tuple.
    3502                 :            :                  */
    3503         [ +  + ]:    3897887 :                 if (relation->rd_rel->relowner == InvalidOid)
    3504                 :            :                 {
    3505                 :            :                         HeapTuple       htup;
    3506                 :            :                         Form_pg_class relp;
    3507                 :            : 
    3508                 :        548 :                         htup = SearchSysCache(RELOID,
    3509                 :        548 :                                                            ObjectIdGetDatum(RelationGetRelid(relation)),
    3510                 :            :                                                                   0, 0, 0);
    3511         [ -  + ]:        548 :                         if (!HeapTupleIsValid(htup))
    3512                 :          0 :                                 elog(FATAL, "cache lookup failed for relation %u",
    3513                 :            :                                          RelationGetRelid(relation));
    3514                 :        548 :                         relp = (Form_pg_class) GETSTRUCT(htup);
    3515                 :            : 
    3516                 :            :                         /*
    3517                 :            :                          * Copy tuple to relation->rd_rel. (See notes in
    3518                 :            :                          * AllocateRelationDesc())
    3519                 :            :                          */
    3520                 :        548 :                         memcpy((char *) relation->rd_rel, (char *) relp, CLASS_TUPLE_SIZE);
    3521                 :            : 
    3522                 :            :                         /* Update rd_options while we have the tuple */
    3523         [ -  + ]:        548 :                         if (relation->rd_options)
    3524                 :          0 :                                 pfree(relation->rd_options);
    3525                 :        548 :                         RelationParseRelOptions(relation, htup);
    3526                 :            : 
    3527                 :            :                         /*
    3528                 :            :                          * Check the values in rd_att were set up correctly.  (We cannot
    3529                 :            :                          * just copy them over now: formrdesc must have set up the
    3530                 :            :                          * rd_att data correctly to start with, because it may already
    3531                 :            :                          * have been copied into one or more catcache entries.)
    3532                 :            :                          */
    3533 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:        548 :                         Assert(relation->rd_att->tdtypeid == relp->reltype);
    3534 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:        548 :                         Assert(relation->rd_att->tdtypmod == -1);
    3535 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:        548 :                         Assert(relation->rd_att->tdhasoid == relp->relhasoids);
    3536                 :            : 
    3537                 :        548 :                         ReleaseSysCache(htup);
    3538                 :            : 
    3539                 :            :                         /* relowner had better be OK now, else we'll loop forever */
    3540         [ -  + ]:        548 :                         if (relation->rd_rel->relowner == InvalidOid)
    3541                 :          0 :                                 elog(ERROR, "invalid relowner in pg_class entry for \"%s\"",
    3542                 :            :                                          RelationGetRelationName(relation));
    3543                 :            : 
    3544                 :        548 :                         restart = true;
    3545                 :        548 :                 }
    3546                 :            : 
    3547                 :            :                 /*
    3548                 :            :                  * Fix data that isn't saved in relcache cache file.
    3549                 :            :                  *
    3550                 :            :                  * relhasrules or relhastriggers could possibly be wrong or out of
    3551                 :            :                  * date.  If we don't actually find any rules or triggers, clear the
    3552                 :            :                  * local copy of the flag so that we don't get into an infinite loop
    3553                 :            :                  * here.  We don't make any attempt to fix the pg_class entry, though.
    3554                 :            :                  */
    3555 [ -  + ][ #  # ]:    3897887 :                 if (relation->rd_rel->relhasrules && relation->rd_rules == NULL)
    3556                 :            :                 {
    3557                 :          0 :                         RelationBuildRuleLock(relation);
    3558         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (relation->rd_rules == NULL)
    3559                 :          0 :                                 relation->rd_rel->relhasrules = false;
    3560                 :          0 :                         restart = true;
    3561                 :          0 :                 }
    3562 [ +  + ][ +  + ]:    3897887 :                 if (relation->rd_rel->reltriggers > 0 && relation->trigdesc == NULL)
    3563                 :            :                 {
    3564                 :      53049 :                         RelationBuildTriggers(relation);
    3565         [ -  + ]:      53049 :                         if (relation->trigdesc == NULL)
    3566                 :          0 :                                 relation->rd_rel->reltriggers = 0;
    3567                 :      53049 :                         restart = true;
    3568                 :      53049 :                 }
    3569                 :            : 
    3570                 :            :                 /* Release hold on the relation */
    3571                 :    3897887 :                 RelationDecrementReferenceCount(relation);
    3572                 :            : 
    3573                 :            :                 /* Now, restart the hashtable scan if needed */
    3574         [ +  + ]:    3897887 :                 if (restart)
    3575                 :            :                 {
    3576                 :      53561 :                         hash_seq_term(&status);
    3577                 :      53561 :                         hash_seq_init(&status, RelationIdCache);
    3578                 :      53561 :                 }
    3579                 :            :         }
    3580                 :            : 
    3581                 :            :         /*
    3582                 :            :          * Lastly, write out new relcache cache files if needed.  We don't bother
    3583                 :            :          * to distinguish cases where only one of the two needs an update.
    3584                 :            :          */
    3585         [ +  + ]:      17683 :         if (needNewCacheFile)
    3586                 :            :         {
    3587                 :            :                 /*
    3588                 :            :                  * Force all the catcaches to finish initializing and thereby open the
    3589                 :            :                  * catalogs and indexes they use.  This will preload the relcache with
    3590                 :            :                  * entries for all the most important system catalogs and indexes, so
    3591                 :            :                  * that the init files will be most useful for future backends.
    3592                 :            :                  */
    3593                 :        128 :                 InitCatalogCachePhase2();
    3594                 :            : 
    3595                 :            :                 /* reset initFileRelationIds list; we'll fill it during write */
    3596                 :        128 :                 initFileRelationIds = NIL;
    3597                 :            : 
    3598                 :            :                 /* now write the files */
    3599                 :        128 :                 write_relcache_init_file(true);
    3600                 :        128 :                 write_relcache_init_file(false);
    3601                 :        128 :         }
    3602                 :      17683 : }
    3603                 :            : 
    3604                 :            : /*
    3605                 :            :  * Load one critical system index into the relcache
    3606                 :            :  *
    3607                 :            :  * indexoid is the OID of the target index, heapoid is the OID of the catalog
    3608                 :            :  * it belongs to.
    3609                 :            :  */
    3610                 :            : static void
    3611                 :            : load_critical_index(Oid indexoid, Oid heapoid)
    3612                 :            : {
    3613                 :            :         Relation        ird;
    3614                 :            : 
    3615                 :            :         /*
    3616                 :            :          * We must lock the underlying catalog before locking the index to avoid
    3617                 :            :          * deadlock, since RelationBuildDesc might well need to read the catalog,
    3618                 :            :          * and if anyone else is exclusive-locking this catalog and index they'll
    3619                 :            :          * be doing it in that order.
    3620                 :            :          */
    3621                 :       1212 :         LockRelationOid(heapoid, AccessShareLock);
    3622                 :       1212 :         LockRelationOid(indexoid, AccessShareLock);
    3623                 :       1212 :         ird = RelationBuildDesc(indexoid, true);
    3624         [ -  + ]:       1212 :         if (ird == NULL)
    3625                 :          0 :                 elog(PANIC, "could not open critical system index %u", indexoid);
    3626                 :       1212 :         ird->rd_isnailed = true;
    3627                 :       1212 :         ird->rd_refcnt = 1;
    3628                 :       1212 :         UnlockRelationOid(indexoid, AccessShareLock);
    3629                 :       1212 :         UnlockRelationOid(heapoid, AccessShareLock);
    3630                 :       1212 : }
    3631                 :            : 
    3632                 :            : /*
    3633                 :            :  * GetPgClassDescriptor -- get a predefined tuple descriptor for pg_class
    3634                 :            :  * GetPgIndexDescriptor -- get a predefined tuple descriptor for pg_index
    3635                 :            :  *
    3636                 :            :  * We need this kluge because we have to be able to access non-fixed-width
    3637                 :            :  * fields of pg_class and pg_index before we have the standard catalog caches
    3638                 :            :  * available.  We use predefined data that's set up in just the same way as
    3639                 :            :  * the bootstrapped reldescs used by formrdesc().  The resulting tupdesc is
    3640                 :            :  * not 100% kosher: it does not have the correct rowtype OID in tdtypeid, nor
    3641                 :            :  * does it have a TupleConstr field.  But it's good enough for the purpose of
    3642                 :            :  * extracting fields.
    3643                 :            :  */
    3644                 :            : static TupleDesc
    3645                 :            : BuildHardcodedDescriptor(int natts, const FormData_pg_attribute *attrs,
    3646                 :            :                                                  bool hasoids)
    3647                 :            : {
    3648                 :            :         TupleDesc       result;
    3649                 :            :         MemoryContext oldcxt;
    3650                 :            :         int                     i;
    3651                 :            : 
    3652                 :      22891 :         oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(CacheMemoryContext);
    3653                 :            : 
    3654                 :      22891 :         result = CreateTemplateTupleDesc(natts, hasoids);
    3655                 :      22891 :         result->tdtypeid = RECORDOID;                /* not right, but we don't care */
    3656                 :      22891 :         result->tdtypmod = -1;
    3657                 :            : 
    3658         [ +  + ]:     543663 :         for (i = 0; i < natts; i++)
    3659                 :            :         {
    3660                 :     520772 :                 memcpy(result->attrs[i], &attrs[i], ATTRIBUTE_FIXED_PART_SIZE);
    3661                 :            :                 /* make sure attcacheoff is valid */
    3662                 :     520772 :                 result->attrs[i]->attcacheoff = -1;
    3663                 :     520772 :         }
    3664                 :            : 
    3665                 :            :         /* initialize first attribute's attcacheoff, cf RelationBuildTupleDesc */
    3666                 :      22891 :         result->attrs[0]->attcacheoff = 0;
    3667                 :            : 
    3668                 :            :         /* Note: we don't bother to set up a TupleConstr entry */
    3669                 :            : 
    3670                 :      22891 :         MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcxt);
    3671                 :            : 
    3672                 :      22891 :         return result;
    3673                 :            : }
    3674                 :            :

    3703                 :            : /*
    3704                 :            :  * Load any default attribute value definitions for the relation.

    3706                 :            : static void
    3707                 :            : AttrDefaultFetch(Relation relation)
    3708                 :            : {
    3709                 :      38380 :         AttrDefault *attrdef = relation->rd_att->constr->defval;

    3719                 :            : 
    3720                 :      38380 :         ScanKeyInit(&skey,
    3721                 :            :                                 Anum_pg_attrdef_adrelid,

    3723                 :      38380 :                                 ObjectIdGetDatum(RelationGetRelid(relation)));
    3724                 :            : 
    3725                 :      38380 :         adrel = heap_open(AttrDefaultRelationId, AccessShareLock);

    3729                 :            :

    3733                 :            : 
    3734         [ +  - ]:     557863 :                 for (i = 0; i < ndef; i++)
    3735                 :            :                 {
    3736         [ +  + ]:     557863 :                         if (adform->adnum != attrdef[i].adnum)
    3737                 :     446972 :                                 continue;
    3738         [ -  + ]:     110891 :                         if (attrdef[i].adbin != NULL)
    3739                 :          0 :                                 elog(WARNING, "multiple attrdef records found for attr %s of rel %s",
    3740                 :            :                                 NameStr(relation->rd_att->attrs[adform->adnum - 1]->attname),
    3741                 :            :                                          RelationGetRelationName(relation));
    3742                 :            :                         else
    3743                 :     110891 :                                 found++;
    3744                 :            : 
    3745                 :     110891 :                         val = fastgetattr(htup,
    3746                 :            :                                                           Anum_pg_attrdef_adbin,
    3747                 :     110891 :                                                           adrel->rd_att, &isnull);
    3748         [ -  + ]:     110891 :                         if (isnull)
    3749                 :          0 :                                 elog(WARNING, "null adbin for attr %s of rel %s",

    3751                 :            :                                          RelationGetRelationName(relation));

    3753                 :     110891 :                                 attrdef[i].adbin = MemoryContextStrdup(CacheMemoryContext,

    3755                 :     110891 :                         break;
    3756                 :            :                 }
    3757                 :            : 
    3758         [ -  + ]:     110891 :                 if (i >= ndef)

    3760                 :            :                                  adform->adnum, RelationGetRelationName(relation));
    3761                 :            :         }
    3762                 :            : 
    3763                 :      38380 :         systable_endscan(adscan);
    3764                 :      38380 :         heap_close(adrel, AccessShareLock);
    3765                 :            : 
    3766         [ -  + ]:      38380 :         if (found != ndef)

    3771                 :            : /*
    3772                 :            :  * Load any check constraints for the relation.
    3773                 :            :  */
    3774                 :            : static void
    3775                 :            : CheckConstraintFetch(Relation relation)
    3776                 :            : {
    3777                 :     152171 :         ConstrCheck *check = relation->rd_att->constr->check;
    3778                 :     152171 :         int                     ncheck = relation->rd_att->constr->num_check;

    3786                 :            : 
    3787                 :     152171 :         ScanKeyInit(&skey[0],
    3788                 :            :                                 Anum_pg_constraint_conrelid,
    3789                 :            :                                 BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
    3790                 :     152171 :                                 ObjectIdGetDatum(RelationGetRelid(relation)));
    3791                 :            : 
    3792                 :     152171 :         conrel = heap_open(ConstraintRelationId, AccessShareLock);
    3793                 :     152171 :         conscan = systable_beginscan(conrel, ConstraintRelidIndexId, true,
    3794                 :     152171 :                                                                  SnapshotNow, 1, skey);
    3795                 :            :

    3797                 :            :         {

    3799                 :            : 
    3800                 :            :                 /* We want check constraints only */
    3801         [ +  + ]:     249060 :                 if (conform->contype != CONSTRAINT_CHECK)
    3802                 :        929 :                         continue;
    3803                 :            : 
    3804         [ -  + ]:     248131 :                 if (found >= ncheck)
    3805                 :          0 :                         elog(ERROR,
    3806                 :            :                              "pg_class reports %d constraint record(s) for rel %s, but found extra in pg_constraint",
    3807                 :            :                              ncheck, RelationGetRelationName(relation));
    3808                 :            : 
    3809                 :     248131 :                 check[found].ccname = MemoryContextStrdup(CacheMemoryContext,
    3810                 :     248131 :                                                                                                   NameStr(conform->conname));
    3811                 :            : 
    3812                 :            :                 /* Grab and test conbin is actually set */
    3813                 :     248131 :                 val = fastgetattr(htup,
    3814                 :            :                                                   Anum_pg_constraint_conbin,
    3815                 :     248131 :                                                   conrel->rd_att, &isnull);
    3816         [ -  + ]:     248131 :                 if (isnull)

    3818                 :            :                                  RelationGetRelationName(relation));
    3819                 :            : 
    3820                 :     248131 :                 check[found].ccbin = MemoryContextStrdup(CacheMemoryContext,
    3821                 :     248131 :                                                                                                  TextDatumGetCString(val));
    3822                 :     248131 :                 found++;
    3823                 :            :         }
    3824                 :            :

    3826                 :     152171 :         heap_close(conrel, AccessShareLock);
    3827                 :            : 
    3828         [ -  + ]:     152171 :         if (found != ncheck)
    3829                 :          0 :                 elog(ERROR,
    3830                 :            :                      "found %d in pg_constraint, but pg_class reports %d constraint record(s) for rel %s",
