    3833                 :            : 
    3834                 :            : 
    3835                 :            : /*
    3836                 :            :  * RelationGetPartitioningKey -- get GpPolicy struct for distributed relation
    3837                 :            :  *
    3838                 :            :  * Returns a copy of the relation's GpPolicy object, palloc'd in
    3839                 :            :  * the caller's context.  Caller should pfree() it.  If NULL is
    3840                 :            :  * returned, relation should be accessed locally.
    3841                 :            :  */
    3842                 :            : GpPolicy*
    3843                 :            : RelationGetPartitioningKey(Relation relation)
    3844                 :            : {
    3845                 :      61733 :     return GpPolicyCopy(CurrentMemoryContext, relation->rd_cdbpolicy);
    3846                 :            : }                                       /* RelationGetPartitioningKey */
    3847                 :            : 
    3848                 :            : 
    3849                 :            : /*
    3850                 :            :  * RelationGetIndexList -- get a list of OIDs of indexes on this relation
    3851                 :            :  *
    3852                 :            :  * The index list is created only if someone requests it.  We scan pg_index
    3853                 :            :  * to find relevant indexes, and add the list to the relcache entry so that
    3854                 :            :  * we won't have to compute it again.  Note that shared cache inval of a
    3855                 :            :  * relcache entry will delete the old list and set rd_indexvalid to 0,
    3856                 :            :  * so that we must recompute the index list on next request.  This handles
    3857                 :            :  * creation or deletion of an index.
    3858                 :            :  *
    3859                 :            :  * The returned list is guaranteed to be sorted in order by OID.  This is
    3860                 :            :  * needed by the executor, since for index types that we obtain exclusive
    3861                 :            :  * locks on when updating the index, all backends must lock the indexes in
    3862                 :            :  * the same order or we will get deadlocks (see ExecOpenIndices()).  Any
    3863                 :            :  * consistent ordering would do, but ordering by OID is easy.
    3864                 :            :  *
    3865                 :            :  * Since shared cache inval causes the relcache's copy of the list to go away,
    3866                 :            :  * we return a copy of the list palloc'd in the caller's context.  The caller
    3867                 :            :  * may list_free() the returned list after scanning it. This is necessary
    3868                 :            :  * since the caller will typically be doing syscache lookups on the relevant
    3869                 :            :  * indexes, and syscache lookup could cause SI messages to be processed!
    3870                 :            :  *
    3871                 :            :  * We also update rd_oidindex, which this module treats as effectively part
    3872                 :            :  * of the index list.  rd_oidindex is valid when rd_indexvalid isn't zero;
    3873                 :            :  * it is the pg_class OID of a unique index on OID when the relation has one,
    3874                 :            :  * and InvalidOid if there is no such index.
    3875                 :            :  */
    3876                 :            : List *
    3877                 :            : RelationGetIndexList(Relation relation)
    3878                 :            : {
    3879                 :            :         Relation        indrel;
    3880                 :            :         SysScanDesc indscan;
    3881                 :            :         ScanKeyData skey;
    3882                 :            :         HeapTuple       htup;
    3883                 :            :         List       *result;
    3884                 :            :         Oid                     oidIndex;
    3885                 :            :         MemoryContext oldcxt;
    3886                 :            : 
    3887                 :            :         /* Quick exit if we already computed the list. */
    3888         [ +  + ]:    2051122 :         if (relation->rd_indexvalid != 0)
    3889                 :    1963416 :                 return list_copy(relation->rd_indexlist);
    3890                 :            : 
    3891                 :            :         /*
    3892                 :            :          * We build the list we intend to return (in the caller's context) while
    3893                 :            :          * doing the scan.      After successfully completing the scan, we copy that
    3894                 :            :          * list into the relcache entry.  This avoids cache-context memory leakage
    3895                 :            :          * if we get some sort of error partway through.
    3896                 :            :          */
    3897                 :      87706 :         result = NIL;
    3898                 :      87706 :         oidIndex = InvalidOid;
    3899                 :            : 
    3900                 :            :         /* Prepare to scan pg_index for entries having indrelid = this rel. */
    3901                 :      87706 :         ScanKeyInit(&skey,
    3902                 :            :                                 Anum_pg_index_indrelid,
    3903                 :            :                                 BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
    3904                 :      87706 :                                 ObjectIdGetDatum(RelationGetRelid(relation)));
    3905                 :            : 
    3906                 :      87706 :         indrel = heap_open(IndexRelationId, AccessShareLock);
    3907                 :      87706 :         indscan = systable_beginscan(indrel, IndexIndrelidIndexId, true,
    3908                 :            :                                                                  SnapshotNow, 1, &skey);
    3909                 :            : 
    3910         [ +  + ]:     151259 :         while (HeapTupleIsValid(htup = systable_getnext(indscan)))
    3911                 :            :         {
    3912                 :      63553 :                 Form_pg_index index = (Form_pg_index) GETSTRUCT(htup);
    3913                 :            : 
    3914                 :            :                 /* Add index's OID to result list in the proper order */
    3915                 :      63553 :                 result = insert_ordered_oid(result, index->indexrelid);
    3916                 :            : 
    3917                 :            :                 /* Check to see if it is a unique, non-partial btree index on OID */
    3918 [ +  + ][ +  - ]:      63553 :                 if (IndexIsValid(index) &&
    3919         [ +  + ]:      63549 :                         index->indnatts == 1 &&
    3920         [ +  + ]:      32283 :                         index->indisunique &&
    3921         [ +  + ]:      13100 :                         index->indkey.values[0] == ObjectIdAttributeNumber &&
    3922         [ +  - ]:       7305 :                         index->indclass.values[0] == OID_BTREE_OPS_OID &&
    3923                 :       7305 :                         heap_attisnull(htup, Anum_pg_index_indpred))
    3924                 :       7305 :                         oidIndex = index->indexrelid;
    3925                 :            :         }
    3926                 :            : 
    3927                 :      87706 :         systable_endscan(indscan);
    3928                 :      87706 :         heap_close(indrel, AccessShareLock);
    3929                 :            : 
    3930                 :            :         /* Now save a copy of the completed list in the relcache entry. */
    3931                 :      87706 :         oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(CacheMemoryContext);
    3932                 :      87706 :         relation->rd_indexlist = list_copy(result);
    3933                 :      87706 :         relation->rd_oidindex = oidIndex;
    3934                 :      87706 :         relation->rd_indexvalid = 1;
    3935                 :      87706 :         MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcxt);
    3936                 :            : 
    3937                 :      87706 :         return result;
    3938                 :    2051122 : }
    3939                 :            : 
    3940                 :            : /*
    3941                 :            :  * insert_ordered_oid
    3942                 :            :  *              Insert a new Oid into a sorted list of Oids, preserving ordering
    3943                 :            :  *
    3944                 :            :  * Building the ordered list this way is O(N^2), but with a pretty small
    3945                 :            :  * constant, so for the number of entries we expect it will probably be
    3946                 :            :  * faster than trying to apply qsort().  Most tables don't have very many
    3947                 :            :  * indexes...
    3948                 :            :  */
    3949                 :            : static List *
    3950                 :            : insert_ordered_oid(List *list, Oid datum)
    3951                 :            : {
    3952                 :            :         ListCell   *prev;
    3953                 :            : 
    3954                 :            :         /* Does the datum belong at the front? */
    3955 [ +  + ][ +  + ]:      63553 :         if (list == NIL || datum < linitial_oid(list))
    3956                 :      58124 :                 return lcons_oid(datum, list);
    3957                 :            :         /* No, so find the entry it belongs after */
    3958                 :       5429 :         prev = list_head(list);
    3959                 :       5429 :         for (;;)
    3960                 :            :         {
    3961                 :       6894 :                 ListCell   *curr = lnext(prev);
    3962                 :            : 
    3963 [ +  + ][ +  + ]:       6894 :                 if (curr == NULL || datum < lfirst_oid(curr))
    3964                 :       5429 :                         break;                          /* it belongs after 'prev', before 'curr' */
    3965                 :            : 
    3966                 :       1465 :                 prev = curr;
    3967                 :            :         }
    3968                 :            :         /* Insert datum into list after 'prev' */
    3969                 :       5429 :         lappend_cell_oid(list, prev, datum);
    3970                 :       5429 :         return list;
    3971                 :      63553 : }
    3972                 :            : 
    3973                 :            : /*
    3974                 :            :  * RelationSetIndexList -- externally force the index list contents
    3975                 :            :  *
    3976                 :            :  * This is used to temporarily override what we think the set of valid
    3977                 :            :  * indexes is (including the presence or absence of an OID index).
    3978                 :            :  * The forcing will be valid only until transaction commit or abort.
    3979                 :            :  *
    3980                 :            :  * This should only be applied to nailed relations, because in a non-nailed
    3981                 :            :  * relation the hacked index list could be lost at any time due to SI
    3982                 :            :  * messages.  In practice it is only used on pg_class (see REINDEX).
    3983                 :            :  *
    3984                 :            :  * It is up to the caller to make sure the given list is correctly ordered.
    3985                 :            :  *
    3986                 :            :  * We deliberately do not change rd_indexattr here: even when operating
    3987                 :            :  * with a temporary partial index list, HOT-update decisions must be made
    3988                 :            :  * correctly with respect to the full index set.  It is up to the caller
    3989                 :            :  * to ensure that a correct rd_indexattr set has been cached before first
    3990                 :            :  * calling RelationSetIndexList; else a subsequent inquiry might cause a
    3991                 :            :  * wrong rd_indexattr set to get computed and cached.
    3992                 :            :  */
    3993                 :            : void
    3994                 :            : RelationSetIndexList(Relation relation, List *indexIds, Oid oidIndex)
    3995                 :            : {
    3996                 :            :         MemoryContext oldcxt;
    3997                 :            : 
    3998 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:         24 :         Assert(relation->rd_isnailed);
    3999                 :            :         /* Copy the list into the cache context (could fail for lack of mem) */
    4000                 :         24 :         oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(CacheMemoryContext);
    4001                 :         24 :         indexIds = list_copy(indexIds);
    4002                 :         24 :         MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcxt);
    4003                 :            :         /* Okay to replace old list */
    4004                 :         24 :         list_free(relation->rd_indexlist);
    4005                 :         24 :         relation->rd_indexlist = indexIds;
    4006                 :         24 :         relation->rd_oidindex = oidIndex;
    4007                 :         24 :         relation->rd_indexvalid = 2; /* mark list as forced */
    4008                 :            :         /* must flag that we have a forced index list */
    4009                 :         24 :         need_eoxact_work = true;
    4010                 :         24 : }
    4011                 :            : 
    4012                 :            : /*
    4013                 :            :  * RelationGetOidIndex -- get the pg_class OID of the relation's OID index
    4014                 :            :  *
    4015                 :            :  * Returns InvalidOid if there is no such index.
    4016                 :            :  */
    4017                 :            : Oid
    4018                 :            : RelationGetOidIndex(Relation relation)
    4019                 :            : {
    4020                 :            :         List       *ilist;
    4021                 :            : 
    4022                 :            :         /*
    4023                 :            :          * If relation doesn't have OIDs at all, caller is probably confused. (We
    4024                 :            :          * could just silently return InvalidOid, but it seems better to throw an
    4025                 :            :          * assertion.)
    4026                 :            :          */
    4027 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:     103544 :         Assert(relation->rd_rel->relhasoids);
    4028                 :            : 
    4029         [ +  + ]:     103544 :         if (relation->rd_indexvalid == 0)
    4030                 :            :         {
    4031                 :            :                 /* RelationGetIndexList does the heavy lifting. */
    4032                 :       1763 :                 ilist = RelationGetIndexList(relation);
    4033                 :       1763 :                 list_free(ilist);
    4034 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:       1763 :                 Assert(relation->rd_indexvalid != 0);
    4035                 :       1763 :         }
    4036                 :            : 
    4037                 :     103544 :         return relation->rd_oidindex;
    4038                 :            : }
    4039                 :            : 
    4040                 :            : /*
    4041                 :            :  * RelationGetIndexExpressions -- get the index expressions for an index
    4042                 :            :  *
    4043                 :            :  * We cache the result of transforming pg_index.indexprs into a node tree.
    4044                 :            :  * If the rel is not an index or has no expressional columns, we return NIL.
    4045                 :            :  * Otherwise, the returned tree is copied into the caller's memory context.
    4046                 :            :  * (We don't want to return a pointer to the relcache copy, since it could
    4047                 :            :  * disappear due to relcache invalidation.)
    4048                 :            :  */
    4049                 :            : List *
    4050                 :            : RelationGetIndexExpressions(Relation relation)
    4051                 :            : {
    4052                 :            :         List       *result;
    4053                 :            :         Datum           exprsDatum;
    4054                 :            :         bool            isnull;
    4055                 :            :         char       *exprsString;
    4056                 :            :         MemoryContext oldcxt;
    4057                 :            : 
    4058                 :            :         /* Quick exit if we already computed the result. */
    4059         [ +  + ]:    3433651 :         if (relation->rd_indexprs)
    4060                 :        407 :                 return (List *) copyObject(relation->rd_indexprs);
    4061                 :            : 
    4062                 :            :         /* Quick exit if there is nothing to do. */
    4063 [ +  - ][ +  + ]:    3433244 :         if (relation->rd_indextuple == NULL ||
    4064                 :    3433244 :                 heap_attisnull(relation->rd_indextuple, Anum_pg_index_indexprs))
    4065                 :    3433076 :                 return NIL;
    4066                 :            : 
    4067                 :            :         /*
    4068                 :            :          * We build the tree we intend to return in the caller's context. After
    4069                 :            :          * successfully completing the work, we copy it into the relcache entry.
    4070                 :            :          * This avoids problems if we get some sort of error partway through.
    4071                 :            :          */
    4072                 :        168 :         exprsDatum = heap_getattr(relation->rd_indextuple,
    4073                 :            :                                                           Anum_pg_index_indexprs,
    4074                 :        168 :                                                           GetPgIndexDescriptor(),
    4075                 :            :                                                           &isnull);
    4076 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:        168 :         Assert(!isnull);
    4077                 :        168 :         exprsString = TextDatumGetCString(exprsDatum);
    4078                 :        168 :         result = (List *) stringToNode(exprsString);
    4079                 :        168 :         pfree(exprsString);
    4080                 :            : 
    4081                 :            :         /*
    4082                 :            :          * Run the expressions through eval_const_expressions. This is not just an
    4083                 :            :          * optimization, but is necessary, because the planner will be comparing
    4084                 :            :          * them to similarly-processed qual clauses, and may fail to detect valid
    4085                 :            :          * matches without this.  We don't bother with canonicalize_qual, however.
    4086                 :            :          */
    4087                 :        168 :         result = (List *) eval_const_expressions(NULL, (Node *) result);
    4088                 :            : 
    4089                 :            :         /*
    4090                 :            :          * Also mark any coercion format fields as "don't care", so that the
    4091                 :            :          * planner can match to both explicit and implicit coercions.
    4092                 :            :          */
    4093                 :        168 :         set_coercionform_dontcare((Node *) result);
    4094                 :            : 
    4095                 :            :         /* May as well fix opfuncids too */
    4096                 :        168 :         fix_opfuncids((Node *) result);
    4097                 :            : 
    4098                 :            :         /* Now save a copy of the completed tree in the relcache entry. */
    4099                 :        168 :         oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(relation->rd_indexcxt);
    4100                 :        168 :         relation->rd_indexprs = (List *) copyObject(result);
    4101                 :        168 :         MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcxt);
    4102                 :            : 
    4103                 :        168 :         return result;
    4104                 :    3433651 : }
    4105                 :            : 
    4106                 :            : /*
    4107                 :            :  * RelationGetIndexPredicate -- get the index predicate for an index
    4108                 :            :  *
    4109                 :            :  * We cache the result of transforming pg_index.indpred into an implicit-AND
    4110                 :            :  * node tree (suitable for ExecQual).
    4111                 :            :  * If the rel is not an index or has no predicate, we return NIL.
    4112                 :            :  * Otherwise, the returned tree is copied into the caller's memory context.
    4113                 :            :  * (We don't want to return a pointer to the relcache copy, since it could
    4114                 :            :  * disappear due to relcache invalidation.)
    4115                 :            :  */
    4116                 :            : List *
    4117                 :            : RelationGetIndexPredicate(Relation relation)
    4118                 :            : {
    4119                 :            :         List       *result;
    4120                 :            :         Datum           predDatum;
    4121                 :            :         bool            isnull;
    4122                 :            :         char       *predString;
    4123                 :            :         MemoryContext oldcxt;
    4124                 :            : 
    4125                 :            :         /* Quick exit if we already computed the result. */
    4126         [ +  + ]:    3433652 :         if (relation->rd_indpred)
    4127                 :        390 :                 return (List *) copyObject(relation->rd_indpred);
    4128                 :            :

    4130 [ +  - ][ +  + ]:    3433262 :         if (relation->rd_indextuple == NULL ||
    4131                 :    3433262 :                 heap_attisnull(relation->rd_indextuple, Anum_pg_index_indpred))
    4132                 :    3433066 :                 return NIL;
    4133                 :            : 
    4134                 :            :         /*
    4135                 :            :          * We build the tree we intend to return in the caller's context. After
    4136                 :            :          * successfully completing the work, we copy it into the relcache entry.
    4137                 :            :          * This avoids problems if we get some sort of error partway through.
    4138                 :            :          */
    4139                 :        196 :         predDatum = heap_getattr(relation->rd_indextuple,
    4140                 :            :                                                          Anum_pg_index_indpred,
    4141                 :        196 :                                                          GetPgIndexDescriptor(),
    4142                 :            :                                                          &isnull);
    4143 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:        196 :         Assert(!isnull);
    4144                 :        196 :         predString = TextDatumGetCString(predDatum);
    4145                 :        196 :         result = (List *) stringToNode(predString);
    4146                 :        196 :         pfree(predString);
    4147                 :            : 
    4148                 :            :         /*
    4149                 :            :          * Run the expression through const-simplification and canonicalization.
    4150                 :            :          * This is not just an optimization, but is necessary, because the planner
    4151                 :            :          * will be comparing it to similarly-processed qual clauses, and may fail
    4152                 :            :          * to detect valid matches without this.  This must match the processing
    4153                 :            :          * done to qual clauses in preprocess_expression()!  (We can skip the
    4154                 :            :          * stuff involving subqueries, however, since we don't allow any in index
    4155                 :            :          * predicates.)
    4156                 :            :          */
    4157                 :        196 :         result = (List *) eval_const_expressions(NULL, (Node *) result);
    4158                 :            : 
    4159                 :        196 :         result = (List *) canonicalize_qual((Expr *) result);
    4160                 :            : 
    4161                 :            :         /*
    4162                 :            :          * Also mark any coercion format fields as "don't care", so that the
    4163                 :            :          * planner can match to both explicit and implicit coercions.
    4164                 :            :          */
    4165                 :        196 :         set_coercionform_dontcare((Node *) result);
    4166                 :            : 
    4167                 :            :         /* Also convert to implicit-AND format */
    4168                 :        196 :         result = make_ands_implicit((Expr *) result);
    4169                 :            : 
    4170                 :            :         /* May as well fix opfuncids too */
    4171                 :        196 :         fix_opfuncids((Node *) result);
    4172                 :            : 
    4173                 :            :         /* Now save a copy of the completed tree in the relcache entry. */
    4174                 :        196 :         oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(relation->rd_indexcxt);
    4175                 :        196 :         relation->rd_indpred = (List *) copyObject(result);
    4176                 :        196 :         MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcxt);
    4177                 :            : 
    4178                 :        196 :         return result;
    4179                 :    3433652 : }
    4180                 :            : 
    4181                 :            : /*
    4182                 :            :  * RelationGetIndexAttrBitmap -- get a bitmap of index attribute numbers
    4183                 :            :  *
    4184                 :            :  * The result has a bit set for each attribute used anywhere in the index
    4185                 :            :  * definitions of all the indexes on this relation.  (This includes not only
    4186                 :            :  * simple index keys, but attributes used in expressions and partial-index
    4187                 :            :  * predicates.)
    4188                 :            :  *
    4189                 :            :  * Attribute numbers are offset by FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber so that
    4190                 :            :  * we can include system attributes (e.g., OID) in the bitmap representation.
    4191                 :            :  *
    4192                 :            :  * Caller had better hold at least RowExclusiveLock on the target relation
    4193                 :            :  * to ensure that it has a stable set of indexes.  This also makes it safe
    4194                 :            :  * (deadlock-free) for us to take locks on the relation's indexes.
    4195                 :            :  *
    4196                 :            :  * The returned result is palloc'd in the caller's memory context and should
    4197                 :            :  * be bms_free'd when not needed anymore.
    4198                 :            :  */
    4199                 :            : Bitmapset *
    4200                 :            : RelationGetIndexAttrBitmap(Relation relation)
    4201                 :            : {
    4202                 :            :         Bitmapset  *indexattrs;
    4203                 :            :         List       *indexoidlist;
    4204                 :            :         ListCell   *l;
    4205                 :            :         MemoryContext oldcxt;
    4206                 :            : 
    4207                 :            :         /* Quick exit if we already computed the result. */
    4208         [ +  + ]:     402818 :         if (relation->rd_indexattr != NULL)
    4209                 :     364920 :                 return bms_copy(relation->rd_indexattr);
    4210                 :            : 
    4211                 :            :         /* Fast path if definitely no indexes */
    4212         [ +  + ]:      37898 :         if (!RelationGetForm(relation)->relhasindex)
    4213                 :      33956 :                 return NULL;
    4214                 :            : 
    4215                 :            :         /*
    4216                 :            :          * Get cached list of index OIDs
    4217                 :            :          */
    4218                 :       3942 :         indexoidlist = RelationGetIndexList(relation);
    4219                 :            : 
    4220                 :            :         /* Fall out if no indexes (but relhasindex was set) */
    4221         [ +  + ]:       3942 :         if (indexoidlist == NIL)
    4222                 :          1 :                 return NULL;
    4223                 :            : 
    4224                 :            :         /*
    4225                 :            :          * For each index, add referenced attributes to indexattrs.
    4226                 :            :          *
    4227                 :            :          * Note: we consider all indexes returned by RelationGetIndexList, even if
    4228                 :            :          * they are not indisready or indisvalid.  This is important because an
    4229                 :            :          * index for which CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY has just started must be
    4230                 :            :          * included in HOT-safety decisions (see README.HOT).
    4231                 :            :          */
    4232                 :       3941 :         indexattrs = NULL;
    4233         [ +  + ]:      10601 :         foreach(l, indexoidlist)
    4234                 :            :         {
    4235                 :       6660 :                 Oid                     indexOid = lfirst_oid(l);
    4236                 :            :                 Relation        indexDesc;
    4237                 :            :                 IndexInfo  *indexInfo;
    4238                 :            :                 int                     i;
    4239                 :            : 
    4240                 :       6660 :                 indexDesc = index_open(indexOid, AccessShareLock);
    4241                 :            : 
    4242                 :            :                 /* Extract index key information from the index's pg_index row */
    4243                 :       6660 :                 indexInfo = BuildIndexInfo(indexDesc);
    4244                 :            : 
    4245                 :            :                 /* Collect simple attribute references */
    4246         [ +  + ]:      17977 :                 for (i = 0; i < indexInfo->ii_NumIndexAttrs; i++)
    4247                 :            :                 {
    4248                 :      11317 :                         int                     attrnum = indexInfo->ii_KeyAttrNumbers[i];
    4249                 :            : 
    4250         [ +  - ]:      11317 :                         if (attrnum != 0)
    4251                 :      11317 :                                 indexattrs = bms_add_member(indexattrs,
    4252                 :      11317 :                                                            attrnum - FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber);
    4253                 :      11317 :                 }
    4254                 :            : 
    4255                 :            :                 /* Collect all attributes used in expressions, too */
    4256                 :       6660 :                 pull_varattnos((Node *) indexInfo->ii_Expressions, &indexattrs);
    4257                 :            : 
    4258                 :            :                 /* Collect all attributes in the index predicate, too */
    4259                 :       6660 :                 pull_varattnos((Node *) indexInfo->ii_Predicate, &indexattrs);
    4260                 :            : 
    4261                 :       6660 :                 index_close(indexDesc, AccessShareLock);
    4262                 :       6660 :         }
    4263                 :            : 
    4264                 :       3941 :         list_free(indexoidlist);
    4265                 :            : 
    4266                 :            :         /* Now save a copy of the bitmap in the relcache entry. */
    4267                 :       3941 :         oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(CacheMemoryContext);
    4268                 :       3941 :         relation->rd_indexattr = bms_copy(indexattrs);
    4269                 :       3941 :         MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcxt);
    4270                 :            : 
    4271                 :            :         /* We return our original working copy for caller to play with */
    4272                 :       3941 :         return indexattrs;
    4273                 :     402818 : }
    4274                 :            : 
    4275                 :            : 
    4276                 :            : /*
    4277                 :            :  *      load_relcache_init_file, write_relcache_init_file
    4278                 :            :  *
    4279                 :            :  *              In late 1992, we started regularly having databases with more than
    4280                 :            :  *              a thousand classes in them.  With this number of classes, it became
    4281                 :            :  *              critical to do indexed lookups on the system catalogs.
    4282                 :            :  *
    4283                 :            :  *              Bootstrapping these lookups is very hard.  We want to be able to
    4284                 :            :  *              use an index on pg_attribute, for example, but in order to do so,
    4285                 :            :  *              we must have read pg_attribute for the attributes in the index,
    4286                 :            :  *              which implies that we need to use the index.
    4287                 :            :  *
    4288                 :            :  *              In order to get around the problem, we do the following:
    4289                 :            :  *
    4290                 :            :  *                 +  When the database system is initialized (at initdb time), we
    4291                 :            :  *                        don't use indexes.  We do sequential scans.
    4292                 :            :  *
    4293                 :            :  *                 +  When the backend is started up in normal mode, we load an image
    4294                 :            :  *                        of the appropriate relation descriptors, in internal format,
    4295                 :            :  *                        from an initialization file in the data/base/... directory.
    4296                 :            :  *
    4297                 :            :  *                 +  If the initialization file isn't there, then we create the
    4298                 :            :  *                        relation descriptors using sequential scans and write 'em to
    4299                 :            :  *                        the initialization file for use by subsequent backends.
    4300                 :            :  *
    4301                 :            :  *              We could dispense with the initialization files and just build the
    4302                 :            :  *              critical reldescs the hard way on every backend startup, but that
    4303                 :            :  *              slows down backend startup noticeably.
    4304                 :            :  *
    4305                 :            :  *              We can in fact go further, and save more relcache entries than
    4306                 :            :  *              just the ones that are absolutely critical; this allows us to speed
    4307                 :            :  *              up backend startup by not having to build such entries the hard way.
    4308                 :            :  *              Presently, all the catalog and index entries that are referred to
    4309                 :            :  *              by catcaches are stored in the initialization files.
    4310                 :            :  *
    4311                 :            :  *              The same mechanism that detects when catcache and relcache entries
    4312                 :            :  *              need to be invalidated (due to catalog updates) also arranges to
    4313                 :            :  *              unlink the initialization files when the contents may be out of date.
    4314                 :            :  *              The files will then be rebuilt during the next backend startup.
    4315                 :            :  */
    4316                 :            : 
    4317                 :            : /*
    4318                 :            :  * load_relcache_init_file -- attempt to load cache from the init file
    4319                 :            :  *
    4320                 :            :  * If successful, return TRUE and set criticalRelcachesBuilt to true.
    4321                 :            :  * If not successful, return FALSE.
    4322                 :            :  *
    4323                 :            :  * NOTE: we assume we are already switched into CacheMemoryContext.
    4324                 :            :  */

    4326                 :            : load_relcache_init_file(bool shared)
    4327                 :            : {
    4328                 :            :         FILE       *fp;
    4329                 :            :         char            initfilename[MAXPGPATH];

    4335                 :            :                                 nailed_indexes,
    4336                 :            :                                 magic;

    4338                 :            : 
    4339         [ +  + ]:      35395 :         if (shared)
    4340                 :      17712 :                 snprintf(initfilename, sizeof(initfilename), "global/%s",
    4341                 :            :                                  RELCACHE_INIT_FILENAME);
    4342                 :            :         else
    4343                 :      17683 :                 snprintf(initfilename, sizeof(initfilename), "%s/%s",
    4344                 :            :                                  DatabasePath, RELCACHE_INIT_FILENAME);
    4345                 :            : 
    4346                 :      35395 :         fp = AllocateFile(initfilename, PG_BINARY_R);

    4348                 :        150 :                 return false;

    4350                 :            :         /*

    4354                 :            :          */

    4675                 :            : 
    4676                 :            : /*
    4677                 :            :  * Write out a new initialization file with the current contents
    4678                 :            :  * of the relcache.
    4679                 :            :  */
    4680                 :            : static void
    4681                 :            : write_relcache_init_file(bool shared)
    4682                 :            : {
    4683                 :            :         FILE       *fp;
    4684                 :            :         char            tempfilename[MAXPGPATH];
    4685                 :            :         char            finalfilename[MAXPGPATH];
    4686                 :            :         int                     magic;
    4687                 :            :         HASH_SEQ_STATUS status;
    4688                 :            :         RelIdCacheEnt *idhentry;
    4689                 :            :         MemoryContext oldcxt;
    4690                 :            :         int                     i;
    4691                 :            : 
    4692                 :            :         /*
    4693                 :            :          * We must write a temporary file and rename it into place. Otherwise,
    4694                 :            :          * another backend starting at about the same time might crash trying to
    4695                 :            :          * read the partially-complete file.
    4696                 :            :          */
    4697         [ +  + ]:        256 :         if (shared)
    4698                 :            :         {
    4699                 :        128 :                 snprintf(tempfilename, sizeof(tempfilename), "global/%s.%d",
    4700                 :            :                                  RELCACHE_INIT_FILENAME, MyProcPid);
    4701                 :        128 :                 snprintf(finalfilename, sizeof(finalfilename), "global/%s",
    4702                 :            :                                  RELCACHE_INIT_FILENAME);
    4703                 :        128 :         }

    4876                 :            : /*
    4877                 :            :  * Detect whether a given relation (identified by OID) is one of the ones
    4878                 :            :  * we store in the init file.
    4879                 :            :  *
    4880                 :            :  * Note that we effectively assume that all backends running in a database
    4881                 :            :  * would choose to store the same set of relations in the init file;
    4882                 :            :  * otherwise there are cases where we'd fail to detect the need for an init
    4883                 :            :  * file invalidation.  This does not seem likely to be a problem in practice.
    4884                 :            :  */
    4885                 :            : bool
    4886                 :            : RelationIdIsInInitFile(Oid relationId)
    4887                 :            : {
    4888                 :    3021456 :         return list_member_oid(initFileRelationIds, relationId);
    4889                 :            : }
    4890                 :            : 
    4891                 :            : /*
    4892                 :            :  * Invalidate (remove) the init file during commit of a transaction that
    4893                 :            :  * changed one or more of the relation cache entries that are kept in the
    4894                 :            :  * local init file.
    4895                 :            :  *
    4896                 :            :  * To be safe against concurrent inspection or rewriting of the init file,
    4897                 :            :  * we must take RelCacheInitLock, then remove the old init file, then send
    4898                 :            :  * the SI messages that include relcache inval for such relations, and then
    4899                 :            :  * release RelCacheInitLock.  This serializes the whole affair against
    4900                 :            :  * write_relcache_init_file, so that we can be sure that any other process
    4901                 :            :  * that's concurrently trying to create a new init file won't move an
    4902                 :            :  * already-stale version into place after we unlink.  Also, because we unlink
    4903                 :            :  * before sending the SI messages, a backend that's currently starting cannot
    4904                 :            :  * read the now-obsolete init file and then miss the SI messages that will
    4905                 :            :  * force it to update its relcache entries.  (This works because the backend
    4906                 :            :  * startup sequence gets into the sinval array before trying to load the init
    4907                 :            :  * file.)
    4908                 :            :  *
    4909                 :            :  * We take the lock and do the unlink in RelationCacheInitFilePreInvalidate,
    4910                 :            :  * then release the lock in RelationCacheInitFilePostInvalidate.  Caller must
    4911                 :            :  * send any pending SI messages between those calls.
    4912                 :            :  *
    4913                 :            :  * Notice this deals only with the local init file, not the shared init file.
    4914                 :            :  * The reason is that there can never be a "significant" change to the
    4915                 :            :  * relcache entry of a shared relation; the most that could happen is
    4916                 :            :  * updates of noncritical fields such as relpages/reltuples.  So, while
    4917                 :            :  * it's worth updating the shared init file from time to time, it can never
    4918                 :            :  * be invalid enough to make it necessary to remove it.
    4919                 :            :  */
    4920                 :            : void
    4921                 :            : RelationCacheInitFilePreInvalidate(void)
    4922                 :            : {
    4923                 :            :         char            initfilename[MAXPGPATH];
    4924                 :            : 
    4925                 :        375 :         snprintf(initfilename, sizeof(initfilename), "%s/%s",
    4926                 :            :                          DatabasePath, RELCACHE_INIT_FILENAME);
    4927                 :            : 
    4928                 :        375 :         LWLockAcquire(RelCacheInitLock, LW_EXCLUSIVE);
    4929                 :            : 
    4930         [ +  + ]:        375 :         if (unlink(initfilename) < 0)
    4931                 :            :         {
    4932                 :            :                 /*
    4933                 :            :                  * The file might not be there if no backend has been started since
    4934                 :            :                  * the last removal.  But complain about failures other than ENOENT.
    4935                 :            :                  * Fortunately, it's not too late to abort the transaction if we
    4936                 :            :                  * can't get rid of the would-be-obsolete init file.
    4937                 :            :                  */
    4938         [ -  + ]:        323 :                 if (errno != ENOENT)
    4939         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         ereport(ERROR,
    4940                 :            :                                         (errcode_for_file_access(),
    4941                 :            :                                          errmsg("could not remove cache file \"%s\": %m",
    4942                 :            :                                                         initfilename)));
    4943                 :        323 :         }
    4944                 :        375 : }
    4945                 :            : 
    4946                 :            : void
    4947                 :            : RelationCacheInitFilePostInvalidate(void)
    4948                 :            : {
    4949                 :        375 :         LWLockRelease(RelCacheInitLock);
    4950                 :        375 : }
    4951                 :            : 
    4952                 :            : /*
    4953                 :            :  * Remove the init files during postmaster startup.
    4954                 :            :  *
    4955                 :            :  * We used to keep the init files across restarts, but that is unsafe even in simple
    4956                 :            :  * crash-recovery cases as there are windows for the init files to become out-of-sync
    4957                 :            :  * with the database. So now we just remove them during startup and expect the
    4958                 :            :  * first backend launch to rebuild them. Of course, this has to happen in each
    4959                 :            :  * database of the cluster.
    4960                 :            :  */
    4961                 :            : void
    4962                 :            : RelationCacheInitFileRemove(void)
    4963                 :            : {
    4964                 :            :         char            path[MAXPGPATH];
    4965                 :            : 
    4966                 :            :         /*
    4967                 :            :          * We zap the shared cache file too.  In theory it can't get out of sync
    4968                 :            :          * enough to be a problem, but in data-corruption cases, who knows ...
    4969                 :            :          */
    4970                 :         12 :         snprintf(path, sizeof(path), "global/%s",
    4971                 :            :                          RELCACHE_INIT_FILENAME);
    4972                 :         12 :         unlink_initfile(path);
    4973                 :            : 
    4974                 :            :         /* Scan everything in the default tablespace */
    4975                 :         12 :         RelationCacheInitFileRemoveInDir("base");
    4976                 :         12 : }
    4977                 :            :

    4991                 :            :                          tblspcpath);
    4992                 :          0 :                 return;

    4995         [ +  + ]:        120 :         while ((de = ReadDir(dir, tblspcpath)) != NULL)

    4999                 :            :                         /* Try to remove the init file in each database */
    5000                 :         84 :                         snprintf(initfilename, sizeof(initfilename), "%s/%s/%s",
    5001                 :            :                                          tblspcpath, de->d_name, RELCACHE_INIT_FILENAME);
